### Laudy Cincotta — 2021-12-06T06:51:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/naggus-journal.26601/post-307150>

> [Naghman said:](/goto/post?id=307112)
>
> Need advice on water filtration. UK will soon start adding flouride to water supply. Is reverse osmosis the only waterfiltration system that will remove fluoride ?
>
> I can set up Reverse osmosis for drinking water but how do I set up reverse osmosis for bathing and shower ? how do I minimise water wastage with reverse osmosis?
>
> is it a waste of time ? Should I just get a standard water filter for drinking water and use deuterium depleted water in addition for drinking and use Lugols iodine to detox flouride?

[@Naghman](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22384/) RO is good but costly to have it plumbed in, also and no way to minimise water wastage which is the downside of RO.

I have three suggestions for you that should keep you covered at all times and fairly inexpensively.

Suggestion 1: Drink DDW alongside imported water ie San Pellegrino/Acqua Panna or Evian or equivalent for health.

Suggestion 2: Get yourself a shower filter that eliminates fluoride and chlorine, this is an example of one in Australia that eliminates chlorine [https://watershop.com.au/product-category/water-filter-systems/sprite-shower-filters/](https://watershop.com.au/product-category/water-filter-systems/sprite-shower-filters/) or something like this that removes both chlorine & fluoride [https://www.aussieberkeyshop.com.au/propur-promax-fluoride-chlorine-reduction-shower-f~4077](https://www.aussieberkeyshop.com.au/propur-promax-fluoride-chlorine-reduction-shower-f~4077).

You ideally want both eliminated and dont want to be breathing in chloramine fumes in the shower, very important.

Some easy research in your area should find you something, pretty sure they are fairly universal.

Suggestion 3: I purchased recently this 4 stage mobile RO from Acqua Tru which I really like, its a bit slow but no wastage and its mobile so take it anywhere with you for benefits.

[https://www.aquatruwater.com](https://www.aquatruwater.com)

The company are also transparent too with their testing results which is always a bonus.

It eliminates Lead, Chlorine, Fluoride, Nitrates, PFOS, PFOA, some Pharmaceuticals found in water, Pesticides and Heavy Metals.

[https://az686452.vo.msecnd.net/cmr/t831/src/0ac675ce5f72425bb0decee5de7c56d5\_1.pdf](https://az686452.vo.msecnd.net/cmr/t831/src/0ac675ce5f72425bb0decee5de7c56d5_1.pdf)

Added:
Finally I forgot to say that RO water is 'dead' water ie mineral devoid because RO process strips it of everything.. you will need to remineralise the water, there are various ways to do this, more than I probably know of, however I use and really like Quinton Ampules. They are deep sea water minerals, I add one to each bottle of water I drink of the stronger ones (dark blue box) and I use the lighter blue box when I feel a bit tired or run down and take one of those straight.

Link is to an awesome study about the benefits of deep sea water I found earlier this year [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/labs/pmc/articles/PMC5221345/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/labs/pmc/articles/PMC5221345/)

Good Luck

### JanSz — 2021-12-06T16:00:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/naggus-journal.26601/post-307167>

> [Laudy Cincotta said:](/goto/post?id=307150)

> [@Naghman](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22384/) RO is good but costly to have it plumbed in, also and no way to minimise water wastage which is the downside of RO.

> I have three suggestions for you that should keep you covered at all times and fairly inexpensively.
>
> Suggestion 1: Drink DDW alongside imported water ie San Pellegrino/Acqua Panna or Evian or equivalent for health.
>
> Suggestion 2: Get yourself a shower filter that eliminates fluoride and chlorine, this is an example of one in Australia that eliminates chlorine [https://watershop.com.au/product-category/water-filter-systems/sprite-shower-filters/](https://watershop.com.au/product-category/water-filter-systems/sprite-shower-filters/) or something like this that removes both chlorine & fluoride [https://www.aussieberkeyshop.com.au/propur-promax-fluoride-chlorine-reduction-shower-f~4077](https://www.aussieberkeyshop.com.au/propur-promax-fluoride-chlorine-reduction-shower-f~4077).
>
> You ideally want both eliminated and dont want to be breathing in chloramine fumes in the shower, very important.
>
> Some easy research in your area should find you something, pretty sure they are fairly universal.
>
> Suggestion 3: I purchased recently this 4 stage mobile RO from Acqua Tru which I really like, its a bit slow but no wastage and its mobile so take it anywhere with you for benefits.
>
> [https://www.aquatruwater.com](https://www.aquatruwater.com)
>
> The company are also transparent too with their testing results which is always a bonus.
>
> It eliminates Lead, Chlorine, Fluoride, Nitrates, PFOS, PFOA, some Pharmaceuticals found in water, Pesticides and Heavy Metals.
>
> [https://az686452.vo.msecnd.net/cmr/t831/src/0ac675ce5f72425bb0decee5de7c56d5\_1.pdf](https://az686452.vo.msecnd.net/cmr/t831/src/0ac675ce5f72425bb0decee5de7c56d5_1.pdf)
>

> Finally I forgot to say that RO water is 'dead' water ie mineral devoid because RO process strips it of everything.. you will need to remineralise the water, there are various ways to do this, more than I probably know of, however I use and really like Quinton Ampules. They are deep sea water minerals, I add one to each bottle of water I drink of the stronger ones (dark blue box) and I use the lighter blue box when I feel a bit tired or run down and take one of those straight.
>
> Link is to an awesome study about the benefits of deep sea water I found earlier this year [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/labs/pmc/articles/PMC5221345/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/labs/pmc/articles/PMC5221345/)
>
> Good Luck

To make water suitable for coral/reef aquarium
RO water is used (that is the dead water you mentioned above)
then
that RO water is mixed with proper amount of (easily available) buckets of sea salt (made create "sea water" in aquariums.
That results in good quality sea water with all the minerals corals expect (they are very sensitive).

Now the question
how to remove the excess of NaCl from that "sea water" (and keep all else as is) so it would be suitable for human consumption.

.

hi JanSz

my initial issue started when I had very mild headstand neck injury

since then I wake up feeling unrefreshed

over the years my hypermobility got worse and so did my posture

what is not helping my posture is stiff, cramping and tight muscles.

I would like to try this Dr Ettinger protocol

I used to get muscle tightness and cramps with exercise only but now I get muscle cramps even if I move my joints in awkward position without exercise.

I have most of the comments u made on potassium bicarbonate
I have tried different types of magnesium in past and most lead to loose stools.

I do have oesophageal reflux sometimes. I get postural hypotension if I get up too quickly in hot sauna room. My blood pressure is usually on the low to normal side.

Should I follow the full Dr Ettinger protocol u posted ?

or modify the potassium bicarbonate to potassium chloride ?

let me know what you guys think ?

it will b nice to sort out muscle cramps as this is the main driver of muscle stiffness and poor posture . I get fatigued, hence unable to hold on to the posture when tired or all long day or working.

### Naghman — 2022-02-14T15:56:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/naggus-journal.26601/post-310037>

hi all

when I eat certain spices, chilly or carbs I feel sleepy and brain fog . This can last 5 to 30 mins. Is this histamine issue?

### John Schumacher — 2022-02-15T17:45:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/naggus-journal.26601/post-310096>

> [Naghman said:](/goto/post?id=310037)
>
> hi all
>
> when I eat certain spices, chilly or carbs I feel sleepy and brain fog . This can last 5 to 30 mins. Is this histamine issue?

Most people have issues with lectins; most spices for chilly are very high in these lectins and mycotoxins; all chilly foods are very high in both lectin and mycotoxin compounds. People who are sensitive to these compounds will experience sleepiness and brain fog quickly after eating them. Histamine is your body's defense mechanism against these compounds.

Please consider eating the PaleoRx prescription by Dr. [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)

[https://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-6-epi-paleo-rx](https://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-6-epi-paleo-rx)
[https://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-5-paradigm-drifts-paradigm-shifts-epi-paleo/](https://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-5-paradigm-drifts-paradigm-shifts-epi-paleo/) ​

>
> **Thanks**

Hey [@Naghman](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22384/) well done!

Are you looking for a permanent fixed under sink system or for a portable one to take with you when moving around?

Both look good.

I have the acqua true portable bench top unit and am happy with it. I haven't had any experience with the Water Revival System.

I do like the thought and thoroughness that the Water Revival System had put into their unit.

Stage 1 and its the use of polypropylene fibres are concerning to me and one which I would investigate further.

Polypropylene is said to be the safest of all plastics because no concerns have yet appeared since its inception yet remembering it does take decades for adverse effects to appear.

I would also consider it worthy of further investigation due to the concerns around phthalates in plastics and their status as known endocrine disruptors.

Polypropylene can sometimes, depending on manufacture also contain toxins such as DEHP which are cancer causing in larger quantities.

If you like this system I would do some more due diligence and speak with the manufacturer asking these questions about the materials used.

They seem very transparent and I like that they have shown their certificate of analysis indicating what is removed by their system.

[https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0580/4717/files/WRS\_CERTFIICATE\_OF\_ANALYSIS.PDF?278](https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0580/4717/files/WRS_CERTFIICATE_OF_ANALYSIS.PDF?278)

The other question I would want answered about the Water Revival System is 'what is the stage 4 high efficiency reverse osmosis membrane made from'?

Acqua Tru is NSF certified and also transparent with their testing of contaminants [https://aquatruwater.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/6451b580990a4994b5886199efed50ed\_1.pdf](https://aquatruwater.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/6451b580990a4994b5886199efed50ed_1.pdf) however don’t say what materials their filters are made of.

I would ask this of the company and then decide because both systems sound really good and I think you will be hard pressed to find any better.

It is only wasted if you don't use it. Water trees around your place to have heathier trees and better block emf, water the garden and lawn to have a better lawn and garden and ground better. [@Naghman](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22384/) sounds interesting so i went and looked around because to my knowledge all RO systems do all produce waste water.

I found this - 'Most reverse osmosis systems waste **as much as 20 gallons** just to produce 1 gallon of product water. The new technology called "ZeroWaste" eliminates this problem by returning the concentrate water from the reverse osmosis system back to the home's plumbing, resulting in 100% efficiency'.

I don't know how that would go and they are not transparent at all, just talk alot about how good they are.

I do love [@Marko Pollo](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/21456/)'s suggestion of how to deal with the waste and would personally go with the traditional system just because i don't know enough about this zero waste business.

I am always sceptical that if it sounds too good to be true then it probably is, requires more investigation.

Good luck with choosing.

# Thread: Winter copulation..........Yes or No?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-copulation-yes-or-no.26605/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2021-11-11T15:16:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-copulation-yes-or-no.26605/post-306044>

Today's pre-winter PSA: Are you ready to now get interested in whether light plays a role in the onset of menopause....?????
"Researchers have found evidence of the effects of season on the activity of the pituitary–ovarian axis and the pineal gland. Kauppila, Kivela, Pakarinen, and Vakkuri (1987) reported an inverse seasonal relationship between melatonin and estradiol secretion observed in 32 Finnish women. The dark season (i.e., winter) was characterized by prolonged and increased melatonin secretion and decreased ovarian and androgenic activities. To further evaluate this association, Kivela, Kauppila, Ylostalo, Vakkuri, and Leppaluoto (1988) measured daytime and nighttime concentrations of serum melatonin, follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH), LH, and prolactin by radioimmunoassay on various days of the menstrual cycle during summer (average daylight 22 hr) and winter (average daylight 5 hr) in healthy women (N = 12) from northern Finland. Kivela and colleagues suggest that in winter, the high levels of melatonin in the follicular phase have an inhibitory effect on the serum LH levels. In summer, the melatonin levels are lower and perhaps less inhibitory on the secretion of LH."
So Uncle Jack, in Winter, should we be copulating according to how our photopic retina was created by Mother Nature? Or rather, why doesn't nature accommodate this behavior so readily when the sun is closer to us, but light's angle of inclination is highly variable to Earth?
Food gurus and supplement makers and infertility docs will sell you drugs.......They love to blame food and use supplements for what light really causes. Do you accept that small-minded thinking or do you choose to go deeper for fundamental truth?
Nope, it's saying darkness (LACK OF ALAN) helped infertile women to conceive. So fascinating, the effect of light on women, don't you think? Might this be why infertility is exploding due to an overdosing of ALAN = artificial light at night?
The MD infertility guru will say: "The COMT enzyme controls the offload of both estrogen and the balance of your neurotransmitters. In the luteal phase, you have high hormone surges and this enzyme is stressed. If you have an SNP in the enzyme more issues can be possible if your circadian rhythms are off. Remember menses are controlled by the central nervous system Supra Chiasmatic Nucleus - it tells the ovaries what to do. Keep the cycle in sync - watch the diet- know your COMT- and the mood menses balance will follow."
Uncle Jack will always chuckle at such functional medical nonsense.
The Quantum Thermodynamic series on Patreon talked about how methionine links to methylation patterns. It amazes me how people do not connect the words of the Patreon blogs to the ideas on social media. The current Quantum engineering series tells you why winter copulation has caveats. Do you know them? Do you know why it happens? [https://www.patreon.com/posts/quantum-3-light-58493297](https://www.patreon.com/posts/quantum-3-light-58493297?fbclid=IwAR31qUdNdNBMSkkqT8uSdCaH8B9775hTU8HrHJQEWf5zBWyYr4IvQHuhIFE)

And homes with energy-efficient lights (LED) get 2x as much melanopsin & [#melatonin](https://www.facebook.com/hashtag/melatonin?__eep__=6&__cft__[0]=AZVuAYDWBGzrSgjzPzoP7FXaHB5NkOIm6uVv6_11HFCzPWHQsNDBjbkCXqQoTnRLfz0ywwSV45jbe_t2Z2-HoKxpJLszw4R9EM2lL-mez0L85wP8fqPtnGVmkJ0RxXT7SPFcUByi6Ww27LJMK2GX6-6vWoEs0r95PRqf7nsjEqutBw&__tn__=*NK*F) & [#sleep](https://www.facebook.com/hashtag/sleep?__eep__=6&__cft__[0]=AZVuAYDWBGzrSgjzPzoP7FXaHB5NkOIm6uVv6_11HFCzPWHQsNDBjbkCXqQoTnRLfz0ywwSV45jbe_t2Z2-HoKxpJLszw4R9EM2lL-mez0L85wP8fqPtnGVmkJ0RxXT7SPFcUByi6Ww27LJMK2GX6-6vWoEs0r95PRqf7nsjEqutBw&__tn__=*NK*F) disrupting light as homes with incandescent light. [#ALAN](https://www.facebook.com/hashtag/alan?__eep__=6&__cft__[0]=AZVuAYDWBGzrSgjzPzoP7FXaHB5NkOIm6uVv6_11HFCzPWHQsNDBjbkCXqQoTnRLfz0ywwSV45jbe_t2Z2-HoKxpJLszw4R9EM2lL-mez0L85wP8fqPtnGVmkJ0RxXT7SPFcUByi6Ww27LJMK2GX6-6vWoEs0r95PRqf7nsjEqutBw&__tn__=*NK*F)[#ArtificialLightAtNight](https://www.facebook.com/hashtag/artificiallightatnight?__eep__=6&__cft__[0]=AZVuAYDWBGzrSgjzPzoP7FXaHB5NkOIm6uVv6_11HFCzPWHQsNDBjbkCXqQoTnRLfz0ywwSV45jbe_t2Z2-HoKxpJLszw4R9EM2lL-mez0L85wP8fqPtnGVmkJ0RxXT7SPFcUByi6Ww27LJMK2GX6-6vWoEs0r95PRqf7nsjEqutBw&__tn__=*NK*F) [#bluelight](https://www.facebook.com/hashtag/bluelight?__eep__=6&__cft__[0]=AZVuAYDWBGzrSgjzPzoP7FXaHB5NkOIm6uVv6_11HFCzPWHQsNDBjbkCXqQoTnRLfz0ywwSV45jbe_t2Z2-HoKxpJLszw4R9EM2lL-mez0L85wP8fqPtnGVmkJ0RxXT7SPFcUByi6Ww27LJMK2GX6-6vWoEs0r95PRqf7nsjEqutBw&__tn__=*NK*F). [https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-020-75622-4](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-020-75622-4?fbclid=IwAR1vDh0cDtUQKqY1qGOS6S1CfkOx7zJoWehff2FyfRyAQr-mAKzK8pE0bvY) THIS IS HOW HUMAN DISEASE BEGINS AND GETS EXTENDED AND WHY DOCS ARE IMPOTENT TO HELP.

### Jack Kruse — 2021-11-11T16:06:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-copulation-yes-or-no.26605/post-306049>

**The effect of seasonal light:**

Google Triathalon deaths or cardiac death in winter so you understand why this is critical. In winter UVA and IRA lights are not present enough. This stresses the melatonin/dopamine/ATP system. You will see that triathlon deaths occur mostly during the swim or immediately after it. Why? Winter cooling has a link. It is the same reason you face your seasonal demise but how it happens in you is a bit different. These athletes are all carb adapted because of their beliefs tied to performance. This changes the protein confirmations on most of the proteins used in the mitochondria. The cytochromes are the most important where quantum tunneling occurs. Once electrons are tunneled they head toward oxygen to reduce it. The inner mitochondrial membrane also has water micelle's around it (MINOS) they have polarized water around it that contain's protons. The matrix has protons (H+ not D) in it that are transferred to the ATPase to make ATP. When they are in a warm environment their proteins have a certain confirmation because they are dehydrated from the task but also because they are living carbs 90% of the time. In a warm environment, it is not a problem for them because they are recycling ATP via the fast pathways you learned about in EMF 4. IR-A light also can augment ATP production. Remember what I taught you in the Energy and Epigenetic series????

As the temperature goes up entropy increases in a system. When entropy increases, water density is less dense. This changes how water can work with sunlight. When water density is less, the water contains less oxygen and fewer electrons. When oxygen is lowered less ATP is made. When oxygen is lowered it causes more DNA methylation defects via TET enzymes. This means that in a warm environment you are running more hypoxic (thiamine link) and you perceive a need for carbs to offset this set of circumstances. Could UVA and IRA light help this? Yes, but this is not always possible during the swim when people have most of their bodies covered up by a suit and sponsors in the colder water. As long as all those conditions stay the same the body and system adapt properly.

When they hit the water or cold temperature they immediately leave a warmer environment during the run or the bike ride........This radically changes the situation of the living system. Remember what I taught you from the CT series. In water,r heat transfers occur 24 times more quickly than they do in the air. The sensory systems in the PNS are all designed from an evolutionary standpoint to respond to **_transient_** **environmental changes and not the status quo.** This is the essence of sensory receptor adaptation in humans.

This is where the shit begins to hit the fan for the athlete or person in the cold when they are carb adapted or not going enough solar exposure. The cold receptors sense the transient change and send it to the brain and the mitochondria in coherent water. (matrix/Cytochrome C) The water surrounds the mitochondrial membrane. This temperature change changes the molecular arrangements of proteins and fats in the outer and inner mitochondrial membrane. It also changes the molecular structure bonding angles and the rotations of the bonds in these molecules. This implies there are electronic, molecular, and rotational changes in these proteins to alter their function in a quantized fashion.

So the body, specifically the mitochondria, begin rapidly to sense this "change in matter" and the proteins and water around the mitochondria react in quantum fashion to this change. But what can not happen to these people? They need ATP to unfold their new protein conformation to bind more water inside the cell to meet the new environmental demands. They can not do it for two reasons. They are dehydrated and they do not have the ability to make enough ATP to match the protein and water changes inside the cell to the radical temperature change, so what happens? They make less ATP and transfer less energy via the water micelles and this all accelerates the process of hypoxia. Another big thing happens. In temps below 62 degrees insulin no longer works in a mammalian cell. If you have no insulin you have no way to work the Krebs cycle to bring metabolic intermediates to the mitochondria. Since all they eat is carbs and protein they have no substrate ready to make ATP. So the cold alone runs them dry of energy substrates to make ATP very quickly because of their mismatched fuel choices. Since they are in cold water they cannot use IRA light to augment this loss either. The cold water hastens this demise in tissue with mitochondrial density. This is why ketosis is linked with cold temps in nature all the time. As it steepens their muscles begin to hurt but eventually it alters their ability to think because they just think this is a temporary thing they experience from the grueling race. Soon their heart/brain becomes starved for O2 while they continue the swim. The environmental signals are massive because of the way heat is transferred in water so rapidly. When they emerge from the water the environment changes just as drastically back to the warm........from cold.

They have an immediate spike in oxygen reduction in the face of cytosol and mitochondria now adapted to severe hypoxia. This is the ultimate environmental mismatch that their body is not prepared for on such a short time scale. It mimics an acute circadian mismatch to light. When this occurs, oxygen becomes toxic as the hydroxyl free radical.......I taught you about this in the Sept webinar of 2013. When this happens it immediately changes the ability to quantum tunnel electrons in the cytochrome proteins because they are loaded with transition metals. This immediately causes the ability to make no ATP. The one bailout would be solar light, but people are covered in triathlons. When ATP is not made in the mitochondria it causes a cell to lose its metastability (read the Q & A October webinar 2013 thread here to get the full impact) and the cell begins to reach true equilibrium. It is no longer able to be far from equilibrium.

When this happens the brain, heart, and skeletal muscles all release water and potassium and become rigid. They can no longer contract and the heart stops in diastolic failure. We call this tissue failure in autophagy. This is precisely how one dies of an AI disease or from sleep apnea. The only difference is the time scales. That last sentence should have caught your attention by design. Light sculpts light and temperature links to the energy and information quanta in light photons. They vary with seasons.

# Thread: Why did a gov't task force change its recs around Aspirin and heart attacks?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-a-govt-task-force-change-its-recs-around-aspirin-and-heart-attacks.26606/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors

### Jack Kruse — 2021-11-12T22:26:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-did-a-govt-task-force-change-its-recs-around-aspirin-and-heart-attacks.26606/post-306096>

The US Preventive Services Task Force recently reversed its position about aspirin- They say, no, you should not take it for primary prevention of heart attack. Might this be tied COVID? Did you know Aspirin has antiviral effects?

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5155651](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5155651)/

This would harm the paradigm beliefs around the mandate.

SARS-CoV-2—the causative agent of COVID-19—is a member of the highly diverse coronavirus family, named for the crown-like spikes present on their surface. Like other members of this virus family, SARS-CoV-2 is an enveloped, positive-sense, single-stranded RNA virus. H1N1 is also a negative-strand RNA virus. Aspirin affects these viruses and is a pretty good antiviral. It is cheap. It has small adverse effects and has a long history. This information is damaging to Big Pharma emergency use authorizations vaccines. Remember, even though the mRNA vaccines have full approval, the EUA vaccines are what people in the USA are still being given. Negative-strand RNA viruses are a group of related viruses that have negative-sense, single-stranded genomes made of ribonucleic acid. They have genomes that act as complementary strands from which messenger RNA is synthesized by the viral enzyme RNA-dependent RNA polymerase.

With respect to this new news around aspirin and its WELL known action with RNA negative strand virus, discord is NOT the great plague of our age. We, scientists and clinicians, need to ask the questions that should be asked so that no one is harmed. I disagree with the premise in the article below.

[https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/should-you-take-aspirin-david-l-katz-md-mph-facpm-facp-faclm/](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/should-you-take-aspirin-david-l-katz-md-mph-facpm-facp-faclm/)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/masks-made-of-silver-and-copper-and-adding-antiviral-oils-to-a-mask-masks-that-are-more-helpful.26608/post-306119>

[https://rumble.com/vobzao-plague-of-corruption-with-judy-mikovits.html](https://rumble.com/vobzao-plague-of-corruption-with-judy-mikovits.html)

32:11 --
Judy Mikovits: "We had our silver masks, which are at least anti-microbial, and we can breathe through them, and we can protect ourselves. There are a lot of people \[exhaling synthetic viral particles from the modified spike protein vaccines, and exhaling other microbes and pathogens\]."

35:15 --
Dr. Darrell Wolfe: "...about where to get the silver masks; I think it's tru47.com, right, Judy?"

[https://tru47.com/collections/masks](https://tru47.com/collections/masks)

[https://tru47.com/collections/masks/products/silver-mesh-cup-mask](https://tru47.com/collections/masks/products/silver-mesh-cup-mask)
SILVER MESH
"- Two layers of 99.99% pure ionically plated silver mesh
\- Silver is known to have antimicrobial, antibacterial and anti-fungal properties
\- Highly breathable
\- Reusable and long-lasting
Disclaimer:
TRU47 masks are not N95, medical or surgical coverings..."

[https://tru47.com/collections/masks/products/heavyweight-mesh-cup-mask](https://tru47.com/collections/masks/products/heavyweight-mesh-cup-mask)
HEAVYWEIGHT MESH
"- Interior and exterior layer of 99.99% pure ionically plated silver mesh
\- Middle layer of jersey modal woven with 99.99% pure silver thread"

[https://tru47.com/collections/masks/products/pure-silver-cup-mask](https://tru47.com/collections/masks/products/pure-silver-cup-mask)
PURE SILVER
"- Highly breathable, however, more dense than our mesh masks"

[https://tru47.com/collections/masks/products/trifecta-cup-silver-mesh-copper-face-mask](https://tru47.com/collections/masks/products/trifecta-cup-silver-mesh-copper-face-mask)
TRIFECTA COPPER/SILVER
"- Interior layer: 99.99% Pure Silver mesh
\- Middle layer: 99.99% Pure Copper
\- Exterior layer: 99.99% Pure Silver mesh
\- Silver is known for its antimicrobial, antibacterial and anti-fungal properties
\- Copper is known to have antiviral properties"

Maybe that one with copper would be useful the way wearing the masks are supposed to be.

\_\_\_\_\_\_

Maybe putting oils with antiviral qualities onto one of those masks would be an improvement.

Blending essential oils and a carrier oil to put in the nose to prevent infection from inhaled spike proteins:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...sy-article-on-prions.25857/page-2#post-300109](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/another-relatively-easy-article-on-prions.25857/page-2#post-300109)

Details of blending essential oils into a carrier oil so they can be safely put in the nose
Second to last link in this post:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...sy-article-on-prions.25857/page-2#post-300344](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/another-relatively-easy-article-on-prions.25857/page-2#post-300344)

One of the things I've been doing to prevent infection from inhaled spike proteins instead of wearing a mask; last paragraph here:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...sy-article-on-prions.25857/page-3#post-304550](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/another-relatively-easy-article-on-prions.25857/page-3#post-304550)

\_\_\_\_\_

Critiques?

### Dan2 — 2021-11-15T22:22:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/masks-made-of-silver-and-copper-and-adding-antiviral-oils-to-a-mask-masks-that-are-more-helpful.26608/post-306250>

I was thinking, because the mesh is less dense than N95, even though most spike proteins would get through still, maybe the ones that do get stuck in the mask would react with the silver or copper to somehow weaken or break the structure of the whole protein, or especially maybe do something to the S1 subunit? And so then even if the particles that do get stuck in the mask are inhaled later, however much it might do that would be a little more useful than other masks that aren't N95. Or I was hoping it could be better than N95 somehow but I don't know how. When some places require a mask and I don't want to have to not go in that place, and even though a lot of people don't want to wear one but the people who own the business and the doorman don't want to deal with someone trying to fine them if someone goes there trying to find a reason to fine them, maybe this would be useful against the spike proteins too to some extent, better than the free disposable ones or buying a cloth one, or ideally better than N95 somehow. And if it is then maybe I'd wear it in grocery stores or other places where they don't enforce it too. So other than the mismatched filtration size -- or let's say then if it was N95 filtration size -- what advantage might there be with the silver or copper?

# Thread: PoW is the circadian clock of a monetary network.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pow-is-the-circadian-clock-of-a-monetary-network.26609/>
**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center
**Posts:** 57
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2021-11-13T14:59:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pow-is-the-circadian-clock-of-a-monetary-network.26609/post-306125>

Proof of stake is a shell game that a marketer introduces to the public to steal their money.

Can I prove this statement using physics? I can.

Everything can and should be thought of as a clock.

How do you create the concept of a singular time if your system spans the galaxy? How do you measure time in a timeless realm? This is a question that life had to ask nature in order to exist. What was her answer to this vexing question?

To answer these questions, we will have to take a closer look at the concept of time itself and how living cells and Bitcoin handle time creation? The physics of organisms creates time for cells. The cellular organization of atoms in the SCN creates time using sunlight from the environment. All life on Earth gets all its energy and information from the sun. Sunlight or the photons that make up the light that falls to Earth are timeless. Light photons never experience time. **_With respect to Bitcoin, money that is used as a token is timeless, but money ledgers are not timeless._**

Bitcoin mimics what cells are doing with light. They consume its energy and information and via the organization of its code it spits out an order as value and time. It creates a ledger of both. How Uncle Jack? Bitcoin makes up its own time: block time — more commonly known as block height. Block height allows Bitcoin to keep track of things: cells and bitcoin are decentralized timepieces that track energy and information. There are two decentralized systems in Nature, one is naturally created by evolution and the other man-made.

Bitcoin is an information ledger that acts as a monetary clock for time and value. Information has to act via entropy because of Claude Shannon's work on information theory. His mathematics in his 1948 paper is a consequence of this idea I am presenting here. Quantum thermodynamics, disciplines concern the flow of information and energy while providing the right language for describing clocks. **All clocks in nature must work by proof of work** to describe information/energy consumption. This is axiomatically true.

PoS cannot accurately control the flow of entropy so they are a poor clock. This means money that uses PoS ideas mimics the problems in paper money.
Nature uses a powerful PoW mechanism:

When you truly get this perspective, it will occur to you that actually, a clock is a thermal machine that creates the illusion of time.

Like an engine, a clock harnesses the flow of energy to do work, producing exhaust in the process. Engines use this energy to propel, and clocks use it to tick. The exhaust in living cells is codified in the CO2 and water appears via transformed atoms by mitochondrial components with the amount of heat generated. Entropy, in the form of energy or information — is the quantity whose incessant rise in the universe is closely associated with the created arrow of time.

**_A clock is anything that undergoes irreversible changes_**: changes in which energy spreads out among more particles or into a broader area. Energy tends to dissipate — and entropy, a measure of its dissipation, tends to increase — simply because there are far, far more ways for energy to be spread out than for it to be highly concentrated.

Since all clocks work by proof of work it should be blatantly obvious to you why you do not want your money to very be denominated in a proof of stake coin. Proof-of-stake does not solve the Byzantine General's Problem. Therefore, it does not provide decentralized consensus. Proof of Stake is an idea human created to try to save energy and information. It turns out that you cannot maintain value or time well if you do not consume energy because, as stated above, _**a money token is timeless, but money ledgers can never be timeless. If they are they lead to the double-spend problem which links to an Oracle issue.
**_
The ledger-like nature of all digital information is the root cause of the double-spend problem. Information never represents the state of the world _directly_. Further, the movement of information implies copying. Information exists in one place, and to “move” it, you have to copy it to another place and erase it at its origin. This problem doesn’t exist in the physical realm where money can exist. In the physical realm, we can actually move money things like tokens or coins from A to B. The informational realm doesn’t have this property. If you want to “move” information from list A to list B, you have to copy it from A to B. Copying information is what a monetary ledger does.

If anyone tells you that any PoS coin is suitable money, and tries to convince you that you should own it, they have just announced to you they are a charlatan or marketer.

Move on from their ideas because they have no fundamental understanding of how time operates in the universe.

If you took this clockmaker's advice in the picture below and bought $10,000 Bitcoin the day the picture was taken a few years ago, it would be worth $292,000 today. That is how you fight the entropy of space/time and Inflation Janet! Jerome Powell and those who will create CBDC will all be bad clockmakers for your health and wealth. Avoid them. The Bitcoin clock ledger is the most accurate clock for a monetary network that calculates time and value in your life.

### Jack Kruse — 2021-11-13T15:51:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pow-is-the-circadian-clock-of-a-monetary-network.26609/post-306132>

What if I told you that this picture represents a clock, would you believe it? We’ll it does. This picture is a blueprint of the Lightning Network. The people who built Lightning are watch makers but few of them realize this. This is a layer two solution created to operate on the Bitcoin monetary network. The Bitcoin network is also a clock. Think about Bitcoin as the hour hand on a wristwatch. Think about the Lightning Network as the minute hand on a wristwatch . The second hand on the wristwatch is the difficulty adjustment in BTC code. The mempool numbers for the Bitcoin network acts like auxiliary timing mechanisms. For example, in watches we have sundials. What are they?

**Subdials** are the mini-dials that sit on the watch face or dial. Also known as auxiliary dials, subdials serve different functions—like tracking lapsed seconds, minutes, and hours, the phases of the moon, a second time zone —across mechanical and specialty watches like chronographs, calendars, and GMT watches.

**_Then you’ll begin to understand why all clocks are flow meters of how entropy flows within a system._**

**_Does this clock above look like anything that looks familiar in Nature to you?_**

### Jack Kruse — 2021-11-13T16:11:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pow-is-the-circadian-clock-of-a-monetary-network.26609/post-306133>

When you stumble upon a time machine and learn how to use it, then you'd be a billionaire. **This is true in money (BTC), and in health (mitochondria).** In Bitcoin, the difficulty adjustment was used by Nakamoto to keep the timing mechanism within the Bitcoin algorithm constant. It was not about a constant level of security, difficulty, or in energy expenditure. **_It was the second hand in the clock timing mechanism of Bitcoin._**

This idea was wise because it follows how Nature’s clocks work in cells. Just as good health has to be costly in time, and not in energy or information, so does money. When you link money to information or energy, this is not sufficient to produce scarcity since every improvement in energy or information generation would allow the system to produce more money. Time is the only thing that the system will never be able to make more of. This makes it appear in reality more like a token and less like a ledger. Time is the ultimate resource of life and money because it is the most scarce asset we have in the cosmos. Because of these thermodynamic ideas of entropy, Bitcoin is the hardest form of money possible because its issuance is directly linked to the ultimate resource of the cosmos……..**TIME**.

### Jack Kruse — 2021-11-13T16:14:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pow-is-the-circadian-clock-of-a-monetary-network.26609/post-306134>

We reserve the name clock, a clock thermodynamicists would realized, for objects whose timekeeping ability is _**enhanced by periodicity**_: some mechanism that spaces out the intervals between the moments when irreversible processes occur. ### Jack Kruse — 2021-11-13T17:02:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pow-is-the-circadian-clock-of-a-monetary-network.26609/post-306143>

Taproot will improve the periodicity of the Bitcoin network's clock timing mechanism. Taproot was built by core BTC developers to improve the Bitcoin timing mechanism. Its added complexity is fuel on the BTC barnfire. In 1948 Claude Shannon paper showed _**that entropy also inversely tracks with information. This means when the core developers improve the complexity of the clock timing mechanism in ANY way, Bitcoin price will go up.**_
_**
Remember Uncle Jack's teaching above:
**_
How do we build better clocks in Nature to increase longevity?

Mother Nature realized 4 billion years ago how to create a better biologic clock. She took the 3 atom clock and improved it by **_adding atomic complexity_** to work with light from the sun 93 million years away from Earth. Bitcoin core developers just did the same to the Bitcoin timing mechanism with Taproot which goes live on November 14th, 2021.

Taproot, the long-anticipated Bitcoin upgrade, will activate this weekend at block 709,632, opening the door for developers to integrate new features that will improve privacy, scalability and security on the network.

The upgrade [locked in](https://www.coindesk.com/tech/2021/06/12/locked-in-bitcoins-taproot-upgrade-gets-its-90-mandate/) back in June, when over 90% of miners chose to “signal” their support. A programmed waiting period between lock-in and activation has since given node operators and miners time to fully upgrade to the latest version of Bitcoin Core, 21.1, which contains the merged code for Taproot. Only once they do so will they be able to enforce the new rules making it possible to use the new type of transaction.

Taproot is a melting pot of various technical innovations throughout Bitcoin’s history into one upgrade. At the root are “Schnorr signatures.” Bitcoin has been using a cryptographic scheme ECDSA for its “digital signatures” where a user signs a transaction with their private key in order to approve sending it somewhere else.

Taproot upgrades to a different scheme called Schnorr. Every transaction using Taproot will now use this new digital signature scheme, adding new capabilities designed to boost the privacy, security and scale of Bitcoin transactions.

In addition to being **smaller and faster** that ECDSA, Schnorr signatures have the added benefit of being “linear,” a combination that will boost Bitcoin’s transaction privacy and allow for more lightweight and complex “smart contracts” (an encoded contract with self-executing rules).

What has Uncle Jack taught you about smaller and faster in quantum thermodynamics? What happens to energy or information in any network when things shrink? They get thermodynamically more efficient. Think about an ankle that become sprained......it goes small to large with swelling. Thinks about a failing heart......it goes from small to large. Think about obesity......a person goes from small to larger. Think about a star dying......it goes from small to large. Any time any decentralize network loses energy or information it undergoes changes in size and shape and this can be measure in how accurate it clock timing mechanism is.

Taproot will have many positive repercussions for various projects across the Bitcoin ecosystem. For instance, multisignature transactions, which require more than one out of a group of signers to sign a transaction, will be cheaper and will use less data. Here you can see how the network become more efficient as Taproot improves the clock timing mechanism in the Bitcoin algorithm

Nakamoto realized the difficulty adjustment complexity was just the Rx to allow value to be accurately transmitted across space/time with no loss of energy or information. Taproot is the idea of Bitcoin core developers to make Bitcoins' SCN a better clock. This improvement can only be done via PoW mechanism. It can never be done via PoS. Taproot is another nail in the coffin of PoS networks.

### Jack Kruse — 2021-11-13T17:05:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pow-is-the-circadian-clock-of-a-monetary-network.26609/post-306145>

How else does improving the clock mechanism at the core of Bitcoin improve the monetary network?

Better Clocks = better privacy.

Taproot increases privacy. Taproot is part of a larger effort by developers around the world on a mission to improve the privacy of Bitcoin, since its transaction history is very public. A curious user can look up any transaction ever sent on Bitcoin using a public block explorer such as [Mempool.space](https://mempool.space/).
This is still the case with Taproot, but details of some more complex transactions (often called “smart contracts”) will be able to be hidden. For example, while right now Lightning Network transactions stand out on the blockchain, Taproot offers the possibility for them to look just like any other transaction, further enhancing transaction privacy. This mean PoS hacks will always continue while they wil become close to impossible when smart contracts are used on the Bitcoin network.

### Jack Kruse — 2021-11-13T17:13:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pow-is-the-circadian-clock-of-a-monetary-network.26609/post-306147>

Think of Taproot like the Cambrian explosion. 650 million years ago the sun got to mid life and began to emit 10% more UV light. Within 50 million years of the quantum mechanical event on the sun complex life showed up on Earth. Why? More UV light = better circadian clock timing = more complex life that could use energy and information more efficiently. It took life 50 million years to build the complexity in cells to take advantage of the sun's new output.

So far, [only a little more than half](https://luke.dashjr.org/programs/bitcoin/files/charts/taproot.html) of known Bitcoin nodes are signaling support for the upgrade. The rest are running old software, which means they will not be able to enforce the new rules of Taproot when it activates – at least, not until they upgrade to Bitcoin Core 21.1. But, even so, the network will still run just fine.

Any miners that have not upgraded to the new software will be unable to successfully mine on the network and they’ll miss out on earning any new block rewards. But developers have done a lot to ensure that miners have had adequate opportunity to get up to speed. Indeed, over 90% of miners have already indicated that they plan to upgrade to the new software, which is why Taproot was able to “[lock in](https://www.coindesk.com/tech/2021/06/12/locked-in-bitcoins-taproot-upgrade-gets-its-90-mandate/)” back in June, and why there was a 5-month delay before activation would kick in.

Activation doesn’t mean that all the work is done, however. Users won’t be able to send or receive the new type of transaction until their particular Bitcoin wallet supports it – and most wallets don’t support it yet. Wallet developers will need to write new code for their wallets to make such transactions possible.

If history is any guide, it could take months or years for wallets to board the train. It took roughly two years for Bitcoin’s last comparably large upgrade, SegWit, to reach [50% adoption](https://charts.woobull.com/bitcoin-segwit-adoption/), for example.

### Jack Kruse — 2021-11-16T16:25:51+0200

Taproot will improve the periodicity of the Bitcoin network's clock timing mechanism. Taproot was built by core BTC developers to improve the Bitcoin timing mechanism. This means when the core developers improve the complexity of the clock timing mechanism in ANY way, Bitcoin price will go up. How will this work with Bitcoin's blockchain Uncle Jack?**_

**_Jevons Paradox_** occurs when technological progress increases the efficiency with which a resource is used, but the rate of consumption of that resource rises due to increasing demand.

As the Bitcoin protocol continues to advance technologically (SegWit, Schnorr, Taproot, OpCTV, DLCs, assumeutxo, Erlay, etc.), individuals will be able to do more creative things with their UTXOs, which should stoke demand for UTXOs, driving up fees. Once block rewards go aways fees are going to be how miners get paid.

What Is UTXO? The term UTXO refers to the amount of digital currency someone has left remaining after executing a cryptocurrency transaction such as bitcoin. The letters stand for **unspent transaction output**. Each bitcoin transaction begins with coins used to balance the ledger.

We're only talking about technological innovations at the protocol level at this point. We haven't even mentioned how Taproot will improve the second layer tech like the Lightning Network and Liquid, which also increase the utility of UTXOs.

**Why is Taproot like the Cambrian explosion?**

It will drive complexity in the Bitcoin ecosystem.

As these particular second layer solutions mature and become more useful to bitcoin users, this will also stoke demand for UTXOs that can be locked up and leveraged in more unique ways, driving up fees.

On top of this, it would be incredibly naive to think that these will be the only second layer technologies that come to market over time. One needs to factor in solutions that haven't even been thought up yet into their long-term Bitcoin security models.

We haven't even touched on the future dynamics of mining, which provides the long-term security that people are so worried about.

As oil and gas companies continue to wake up and realize they can use all of the FREE gas they are currently wasting on their fields to mine bitcoin, this will severely reduce the price at which your average miner can mine profitably. An externality that many are blind to at the moment.

When you combine all of this, it is incredibly stupid to claim with any sense of authority that Bitcoin's long-term security is at risk. The number of things one can do with a UTXO is increasing at an incredible rate. This utility will drive demand, and create a fee market.

Many critics crypto believe Bitcoin's monetary policy is flawed because its security will depend on a healthy fee market one day.
Critics are not very forward thinking, in my opinion, because they are ignorant of things they don't know. They chose to stay ignorant usually because they want to pump their ETH bags.

Their argument revolves around a perceived need for a "minimum viable issuance" \[read: constant inflation rate\] to incentivize miners to mine the chain in lieu of a sufficient block subsidy. This is some very high quality concern trolling that aims to position Ethereum as having a "superior monetary policy" because no one can truly know what will happen once the block subsidy falls below a certain point.

Newsflash, bitcoin was invented to provide the world with a scarce information space that can be used as a sound monetary good. A "minimum viable issuance rate" is against the whole ethos of the protocol and the fight for sound money in the Digital Age. Bitcoin is a bold affront to the conventional thinking on monetary policy that has consumed the world for quite some time now. Those who think constant inflation is necessary are thinking anachronistically. The times are changing, freaks. And this is a good thing.

As the central bankers and politicians of the world continue to erode the confidence of the populaces they rule over, more people will come to value the utility that Bitcoin provides. This will cause more people to use bitcoin and the nature of bitcoin's scarce information space will force a healthy fee market to develop. From these small additions in code, more periodicity comes and as it comes it allows for more Bitcoin complexity.

The BTC UTXO is a commodity that become more efficient with Taproot activation. Moreover, because it makes BTC more efficient it increases the amount of utility it provides the market and demand for that commodity increases alongside that utility increase. It's a paradox because many would believe that as things become more efficient, people use them less. It isn't true. They will be used more.

**Why specifically does Taproot mimic the Cambrian explosion?**

The Taproot activation enables bitcoin users to leverage the benefits of Schnorr signatures and gives code developers the ability to write create more expressive scripts that can enable **_more complex transactions.
_**
This will create a base from which to build better privacy enabling tech in the future, while ushering in material improvements to transaction data efficiency and the functional utility of bitcoin UTXOs. These improvements represent massive jumps that will increase the amount of things a bitcoin UTXO can do. ### Jack Kruse — 2021-11-28T16:01:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pow-is-the-circadian-clock-of-a-monetary-network.26609/post-306753>

BIP (Bitcoin Improvement Proposal).

Here are the main ones that could be integrated into the Bitcoin protocol shortly because of Taproot.

**BIP 118 — SIGHASH\_NOINPUT**
In [the original Lightning Network whitepaper](https://lightning.network/lightning-network-paper.pdf), Joseph Poon advocated the use of SIGHASH\_NOINPUT to be able to create the first commitment transaction that references the txid of the funding transaction without actually signing the funding transaction.

This document was written before the implementation of Segregated Witness (SegWit). Currently, the Lightning Network, therefore, operates without SIGHASH\_NOINPUT as a new hash flag signature. This does not seem to be a necessity at the moment.

However, Christian Decker has since proposed [the eltoo payment channel construction](https://blockstream.com/eltoo.pdf). The latter would get rid of the penalty-based construction that is currently used and thus significantly reduce the overhead of maintaining the channel state since the state becomes symmetric for all participants. In particular, multiparty channels and channel factories would become easier to use.

Eltoo depends on the implementation of SIGHASH\_NOINPUT because the channel state is encoded as a chained list of update transactions that, in case of a protocol violation, are skipped from the point of violation to the current channel state, which requires SIGHASH\_NOINPUT.

All Lightning Network developers agree that Lightning channels would benefit from being built via eltoo. For this to happen, [BIP 118](https://github.com/bitcoin/bips/blob/master/bip-0118.mediawiki) will need to be implemented within the Bitcoin protocol. Like Taproot, it’s not the development itself that is most anticipated, but what it will enable next.

**BIP 119 — OP\_CHECKTEMPLATEVERIFY**
[BIP 119](https://github.com/bitcoin/bips/blob/master/bip-0119.mediawiki) aims to incorporate a new opcode that will be used for transaction congestion control and payment channel instantiation primarily. The opcode OP\_CHECKTEMPLATEVERIFY is one of the technical approaches being considered for the implementation of covenants.

Covenants are restrictions on how a corner can be spent beyond key ownership. Covenants can be useful in the construction of smart contracts. They can also be used to further boost the scalability of the Lightning Network in the future.

Complex to implement and comes with a risk of introducing discriminating fungibility, covenants remain in the draft stage for now and have never really been seriously considered for inclusion in the Bitcoin protocol. One technical approach being considered for covenants is based on the opcode OP\_TAPLEAF\_UPDATE\_VERIFY.

**Drivechains**
If you’re interested in the cryptocurrency world, you’ve probably already heard of the concept of “Sidechains”. This is a feature that has long been proposed to be added to Bitcoin. These are additional blockchains that can be pegged to Bitcoin. Some of these sidechains could be used to test new experimental technologies that Bitcoin has not yet implemented.

One can think here of a feature similar to zk-SNARKs used by the Zcash privacy coin. This feature offers Zcash users more privacy than Bitcoin currently offers its users. Users could lock up their Bitcoin to use this new coin in a sidechain.

In addition to the [BIP 300](https://github.com/bitcoin/bips/blob/master/bip-0300.mediawiki) and [BIP 301](https://github.com/bitcoin/bips/blob/master/bip-0301.mediawiki) that he carries, researcher Paul Sztorc has implemented with other developers a working version of this sidechain idea under the name [Drivechains](http://drivechain.info/).

Nevertheless, “Drivechains” represents a very controversial idea within the Bitcoin developer community, because some developers say that it could give too much power to the miners of the Bitcoin network. So this feature is not for tomorrow, but it’s good to know that it is one of the ideas that are mentioned from time to time in the Bitcoin world.

**Cross-input signature aggregation**
The activation of Taproot paves the way for a whole bunch of new developments on top of the Bitcoin blockchain. This is the case with cross-input signature aggregation (CISA). Digital signatures are a crucial part of Bitcoin. When a user wants to send Bitcoin, he has to sign his Bitcoin using his private keys to prove that he is the owner of that Bitcoin.

Taproot introduces Schnorr signatures, which allow multiple signatures to be combined into one, making transactions a little cheaper while boosting scalability.

Above Taproot, the CISA feature allows signatures in a single transaction to be aggregated. The main consequence of CISA is that it would make CoinJoin transactions cheaper. Used via the Wasabi and Samurai wallets, CoinJoin is a method to boost the privacy of users’ transactions.

With CISA, CoinJoin transactions would become less expensive, which would encourage more users to use them. This would greatly enhance the privacy of the Bitcoin network. CoinJoin would no longer be something you have to pay more for, but the norm.

### Jack Kruse — 2021-11-30T21:10:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pow-is-the-circadian-clock-of-a-monetary-network.26609/post-306898>

**Understanding clock management is the job of the Black Swan Mitochondriacs.**

So in my recent private webinar series I taught my farm members who attended this event about how the loss of negative feedback control in coupled biologic systems is the sentinel event for aging and disease generation. Moreover, I showed them what happens when you lose it in one side of the **_coupled time event_**.

There I used predator or prey to make the point. If you alter the balance of predator or prey the result is always the EXTINCTION of both animals.

I have taught you that in aging and neolithic disease generation that NAD+ becomes altered in relationship to NADH in mitochondria = a bad clock. The chronic loss of NAD+ is the critical sign of a loss of negative feedback control of the ubiquitin cycle = proxy for excessive protein synthesis = more growth. Growth is expensive to mitochondria. Therefore we do not want it if we don't need it. Altered space/time relations always lead to bad protein synthesis issue. Think of my analogies with Home Depot when arrival and delivery's come on the same day. Not bueno.

Now for how this scales to your molecular circadian clock and your peripheral clock genes (CCG's) is the key to being a good clock manager.

The current model of the mammalian circadian clock includes two interlocking transcription-translation feedback loops comprised of several so-called “clock” genes and their protein products, which ultimately regulate the transcription of “clock-controlled” genes. These feedback loops consist of positive and negative components. The positive components include the basic helix-loop-helix-PAS domain transcription factors, CLOCK and BMAL1. These transcription factors heterodimerize, translocate from the cytosol to the nucleus, and bind to circadian E-box promoter elements that enhance the transcription of genes encoding the negative components PERIOD 1 & 2 and CRYPTOCHROME 1 & 2.

The CRYPTOCHROME and PERIOD proteins feedback inhibit the transcription of the Cryptochrome and Period genes by blocking CLOCK/BMAL1-mediated trans-activation. All these gears are used to signal a deformation in the space/time continuum you live in for cells. Amazing how complex cells became after the Cambrian explosion, no? Can you imagine what Bitcoin will look like 25-50 years from now with respect to your money or value?

The second feedback loop involves the trans-activation of the Rev-Erbα and Rora genes by CLOCK/BMAL1. The protein products of these genes compete for binding to RRE elements in the Bmal1 promoter, driving a daily rhythm of Bmal1 transcription and closing the second feedback loop. Rhythmic expression (**HERE IS THAT PERIODICITY ISSUE)** of these clock gene products produces circadian clock outputs by regulating transcription of clock-controlled genes (CCGs).

What did I say about ideal clock management above?

All clocks are flow meters for entropy. Anything that improves the PERIODICITY of the clock improves its ability to tell how time is flowing around you.

Taproot will improve the periodicity of the Bitcoin network's clock timing mechanism so in a way it mimics what Vitamin D for cells.. Vitamin D was a Taproot 420 million years ago for new life moving from the sea to land. In 1948 Claude Shannon paper showed **_that entropy also inversely tracks with information. This means when the feedback loops in cells improve the complexity of the clock timing mechanism in ANY way, life can survive on and because cells have obtained a way to deform the space time around them to harness time in their favor._** Healthspan predicts longevity and species survival. This is how clock management operates in life.

Back to cell clocks: At least some of these CCGs, including aanat, is the gene encoding the penultimate enzyme in the **melatonin biosynthetic pathway. Why is this big? Melatonin controls the process of mitochondrial recycling or replacement. It controls the clock function of mtDNA!!!!**

All of the CCGs in the melatonin pathway also contain circadian E boxes, which have a core nucleotide sequence of CACGTG and are activated rhythmically by CLOCK/BMAL1. This links them to the PERIODICITY OF LIGHT AND DARK!!!!!

Post-translational regulation, including phosphorylation, acetylation, ubiquitinatation, sumoylation and proteasomal degradation are also important in the regulatory mechanisms generating the circadian oscillation.

Once you SCN goes haywire it is a matter of time before your circadian clock genes in tissues the SCN controls goes haywire. What will be the ultimate result? EXTINCTION of both sides of the circadian coupling and diseases like HBP, cancer, and poor sleep result. My PSA for the day is done. It is all about clock management.

### Jack Kruse — 2022-08-07T19:53:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pow-is-the-circadian-clock-of-a-monetary-network.26609/post-314913>

Satoshi wasn't a seller of bitcoin. He was a buyer. Because he had to purchase his coins from nature — with electricity and processor cycles — just like anyone else. There's something so attractive about this: the creator of new money following the same rules as everyone else in Nature.
The contrast to an ICO or pre-mine is so stark, isn't it? "Buy my newly minted magical beans!", say the carnival barkers. Satoshi, meanwhile, quietly mines new blocks, paying all the while (electricity isn't free) for their rewards.
Some nihilistic types claim the insider/early adopter distinction doesn't matter: "We're all late by comparison, so who cares how this stuff started?" Wrong. The \*origins\* of the distribution of goods factor into the \*justice\* of that distribution.
Two snapshot distributions might be identical, in fact, but one just and the other unjust. Why? Because of their histories. When one has its origins in theft or violence and the other does not, for example, one can be just while the other is not -- despite appearing the same!
So also, the origins and history of bitcoin's present distribution very much matter when morally assessing it. Did everyone follow the same rules? Did the creator of bitcoin have to buy coins with energy, just like anyone else?
Just as the creation of life on Earth needed to use the power of the sun to create life.
The argument here for giving moral weight to history and not just a pattern of distribution, incidentally, derives from Chapter 7 of Robert Nozick's classic, \_Anarchy, State, and Utopia\_. A must-read masterpiece in political philosophy.
I’m seeing another kind of nihilistic reply from the ignorant on PoW: since the network was worth so little when Satoshi was around, what happened then doesn’t matter. This thinking is wrong. small features in the deep history of distribution can make a huge difference to its present moral status.

BUTTERFLY EFFECT: small changes lead to a massive rapid amplification of phenotype change
That was proven here: There was ample opportunity for DNA modification for the first 2.5 billion years of life on Earth. Yet, there is no evidence of significant change in life forms during that time span. It was not until about 600 million years ago when the oxygen tension rose to a point where air-breathing life forms became thermodynamically possible and favored, that a significant change can be abruptly seen in the fossil record. In other words, DHA creation and use were critical to allowing our DNA to change. BTC has done the same thing to monetary systems. In biology, optimization of PoW by DHA allowed the sudden appearance of the 32 phyla of living creatures seemingly overnight in the Cambrian fossil record and was also associated with the appearance of intracellular detail not seen in previous life forms.

There’s an additional extrinsic benefit from thermodynamic rules that apply equally to all via PoW, even in a tiny and nascent network. It sets a good precedent and example. And early exemplars and precedents have an outsized effect = Cambrian explosion.

Another example:
Transacting in bitcoin is like crossing a river. if you want to cross the river, you pay a ferry. Anyone can build a boat and start ferrying passengers for the same fee. In PoS, transacting is like crossing private land. You pay the landowner for the right to pass. In this example, only the landowner is enriched by the land crossing. Of course, you too, can charge for others’ land crossings, but you must first buy some land from the land owners. There is no equivalent to building your own boat.
Stakers can’t lose because landowners can’t lose. There’s only one way to cross the land: pay the owners. The owners have no competition. Ferry guides, by contrast, will proliferate along the river bank as long as it is profitable to run a ferry. This is how evolution operates.
Sometimes boats will be bought and built and the fees recovered won’t be enough to cover those expenses, and the boatmen will turn to something else like fishing, diving, or salvage repair.
I hope you see the central point: **_PoS is feudalism_**. The Lord owns the estate. You must pay them, the central controller of the system, until you own your plot of land and grow some wheat on it. Forever. And that lord is the kind of guy who has an ape. Nonstop raves in the castle.

### Jack Kruse — 2021-11-13T21:16:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-the-great-reset-began-in-the-usa-the-patriot-act.26611/post-306160>

On September 16, 2001, Vice President Cheney appeared on NBC's Meet the Press and talked about what it will take to deal with the terrorism threat: "…We have to work the dark side, if you will. Spend time in the shadows of the intelligence world," Cheney said. "A lot of what needs to be done here will have to be done quietly, without any discussion …" Here, intelligence officials and others offer their thoughts on Cheney's use of that phrase, as well as the impact of 9/11 on the vice president.

What VP Cheney was hiding was that he was working for the 8 families of the BIS who control the Fed. These are the very same people who had Gen, Michael Hayden of the NSA cancel bill Binney's algorithm ThinThread which would have cancelled Bush and Cheney's role in helping take down the US constitution for the globalists at the WEF. Hayden was critical to the Cheney plan of creating a crisis to usurp power and get the US Congress to pass the Patriot Act in record time. It was passed in 45 days with only one brave soul in Congress voting no. She was ostracized for it, but she was the only wise person that day.

The Bush administration hand selected their Manchurian Candidate to run the NSA. They chose Michael Hayden.
From 1996 to 1997, Hayden served as commander of the [AIA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Air_Force_Intelligence,_Surveillance_and_Reconnaissance_Agency), an agency of 16,000 charged with defending and exploiting the "information domain." Hayden served the Bush White House and the BIS as the Director of the National Security Agency and Chief of the [Central Security Service](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Central_Security_Service) at [Fort George G. Meade](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fort_George_G._Meade), [Maryland](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maryland) from March 1999 to April 2005. As the Director of NSA and Chief of CSS, he was responsible for a combat support agency of the Department of Defense with military and civilian personnel stationed worldwide. Who was he combating? The People of the USA.

Hayden was elected by Bush for the NSA top job to make sure there was no way for the intelligence agency to pick up metadata to show that the US government helped Saudi nationals take down the Towers on 9/11 to get Congress to pass mass surveillance under the guise of National Security from Terrorism. Hayden made sure billons were spent on the Trailblazer algorithm which was impotent to stop any terrorist from anything.

This program was ineffective on capturing the terrorists plot apriori.
On 9/11, Hayden immediately evacuated all non-essential personnel from NSA headquarters to make sure no one found evidence of a competing algorithm created by Bill Binney called ThinThread. Thin Thread was far cheaper and actually captured all the data on the terrorists groups three weeks before 9/11 happened.

Binneys' data algorithm did capture the data of communications out of San Diego of the coming plot. This story is buried to the public because of an FBI raid on Binney and the NSA Whistle blowers done by the Bush administration in 2007. The FBI case against Bill, Kirk, and Diane was dropped because their defense team was able to prove the Bush White house fabricated evidence so that an illegal warrant could get the ThinThread metadata that proved the US government blinded the NSA by design to facilitate and economic reset and weakening of the US Constitution.

In May 2006, _[USA Today](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/USA_Today)_ reported that, under Hayden's leadership, the NSA created a [domestic telephone call database](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NSA_call_database). During his nomination hearings, Hayden defended his actions to Senator [Russ Feingold](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Russ_Feingold) and others, stating that he had relied upon legal advice from the BUSH [White House](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/White_House) that building the database was supported by [Article Two of the United States Constitution](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Article_Two_of_the_United_States_Constitution) executive branch powers (in which the president must "take care that the laws be faithfully executed"), overriding legislative branch statutes forbidding [warrantless surveillance](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Warrantless_wiretapping) of domestic calls, which included the [Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Foreign_Intelligence_Surveillance_Act) (FISA). Previously, this action would have required a warrant from a FISA court. The stated purpose of the database was to eavesdrop on international communications between persons within the U.S. and individuals and groups overseas in order to locate terrorists. Instead, the NSA and FBI used it against an elected official to incite and insurrection. Abuse of the FISA actions was used against President Trump in 2016 by NSA director Brennan. They tried to blame Russia for the Steele Dossier when it was Hillary Clinton who paid for it to use to capture the election in 2015. The abuse of power was useful because of Hayden's work at the NSA. 9/11 was the largest failure in NSA history because the NSA was not allowed to use Binney's ThinThread and forced to use Trailblazer instead as per the bush instructions.

Hayden also championed the [Trailblazer Project](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trailblazer_Project) on behalf of Cheney and Bush, as a "transformation" project with a large information technology component. The project was criticized by several NSA staffers for not including privacy protections for United States citizens and for being a waste of money. The critics included Diane S Roark, of the [House Intelligence Committee](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/House_Intelligence_Committee), NSA workers [Thomas Andrews Drake](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thomas_Andrews_Drake), [William Binney](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/William_Binney_\(U.S._intelligence_official\)), J. Kirk Wiebe, and Loomis, and others. Hayden severely rebuked these critics. Several quit in protest. After investigations by the NSA inspector general, the DOD inspector general, and Congress, Trailblazer was shut down because it had no ROI for the NSA.

What did Hayden get for this massive failure from the Bush White House? He got a promotion. This is the move of a traitor and criminals in the White House. Bush rewarded his "loyal General" by using the law. As part of the [Intelligence Reform and Terrorism Prevention Act](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Intelligence_Reform_and_Terrorism_Prevention_Act) of 2004, the CIA chief no longer would run the intelligence community. Instead a new office was created for this purpose; the [Director of National Intelligence](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Director_of_National_Intelligence). General Hayden became the Principal Deputy Director of National Intelligence from May 2005 to May 2006 under the first DNI, [John Negroponte](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/John_Negroponte).

On January 23, 2006, Hayden participated in a news conference. A YouTube video was posted of Michael Hayden telling reporters at a press conference that "probable cause" is not required for all searches or seizures under the [Fourth Amendment](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fourth_Amendment_to_the_United_States_Constitution), claiming instead that the standard is whether the search or seizure is reasonable. "Probable cause" is required for all warrants, whether or not the search or seizure is deemed to be "unreasonable." Now you can see why I wrote the BTC #4 blog.
**[https://www.patreon.com/posts/43731572](https://www.patreon.com/posts/43731572)**

On May 8, 2006, Hayden was nominated by President George W. Bush to be Director of the Central Intelligence Agency after the resignation of [Porter J. Goss](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Porter_J._Goss) on May 5, 2006. He was later confirmed on May 26, 2006, as director, 78–15, by full [U.S. Senate](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/U.S._Senate) vote. 78 Senators elected a traitor to run the CIA. Senator [Dianne Feinstein](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dianne_Feinstein) who stated on May 11, 2006, that "I happen to believe we are on our way to a major constitutional confrontation on Fourth Amendment guarantees of unreasonable search and seizure" if we vote for Hayden. She got that right.

Under his "leadership" we were introduced to Julian Assange and Ed Snowden.
In September 2013, Hayden stressed the indisputable legality of "what the NSA is doing" and called [Edward Snowden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Edward_Snowden) a "troubled young man" and "morally arrogant to a tremendous degree"; he also said about his prospects in Russia: "I suspect he will end up like most of the rest of the defectors who went to the old Soviet Union: Isolated, bored, lonely, depressed—and most of them ended up alcoholics." This is what a traitor said about a true Patriot. We are being governed by criminals.

All of this is covered in an excellent documentary called " A Good American". As a result of the Patriot act, this has lead to a global surveillance capitalism movement. Today, COVID is part of that movement. So are vax mandates. So too is the green energy movement and the movement to push veganism as I laid out in BTC #6. [https://www.patreon.com/posts/45194923](https://www.patreon.com/posts/45194923) To date, surveillance capitalism has not prevented one terror attack. It has fueled the systematic destruction of our Founding Documents. Micael Hayden, Maureen Baginski, Sam Visser, and Charlie Allen were the traitors who buried the truth of ThinThread from the public so that the Great Reset might happen in 15-20 years. Today, you are living it. Liz Cheney the daughter of one of the architects of this plan remains in Congress. She is an enemy of the State and hates Bitcoin. Her allegiance is pledged to the families who own the Federal reserve.

**_When you know better, you do better. Moreover, you always do better when you buy Bitcoin to short the criminals in Washington DC._**
