### Inger — 2023-05-04T21:38:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1900-links-for-pbm-studies.26673/post-320870>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=320863)
>
> very interesting Inger - please tell us more about your thoughts on beans and getting rid of excess stress hormones

I will Caroline, I need to do an update in my journal soon. Its just that I am doing it for only 3 months so far so I cant really say much yet but I sure can tell it has helped my sleep and relaxation !
The hypothesis goes that our intestines has a place where excess hormones get re absorbed and that will especially happen if you dont have any soluble fiber in your diet, which I did not. So then if you create too much hormones and they cant get cleared out, its a bad thing for your health. Trauma will also make your liver not work properly. Its a lot to do with bile, its in the bile where the body get rid of hormones and other poisons from the liver. So the soluble fiber soaks up the bile and takes it out in the poop.

It has a name and it is "the bean protocol". You eat animal food, veggies, beans, grains if you tolerate or want them, and beans. No stimulants, no coffee, no sugar. Beans several times / day. And then you need patience, very slowly your bile gets cleaned out. it takes weeks and months. People get rid of anxiety, adrenal fatigue, hormonal disturbances, depression, chrons...all sort of things. Its so crazy, how can beans do this!
One lady in the facebook group told that it took her 2 years, but then she was like a new person.
The cool thing is that beans are super cheap and easy to take to work and I love the taste, especially black beans. I eat them all the time. It takes a while for the gut to adapt to trhem though, if you have not been used to et beans

### John Schumacher — 2023-05-04T22:42:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1900-links-for-pbm-studies.26673/post-320879>

> [Johan Lindstrøm said:](/goto/post?id=320684)
>
> How i use ir and uv light.
>
> [https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/johan-journal.22865/post-320683](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/johan-journal.22865/post-320683)
>
>
> What do you think?

[@Johan Lindstrøm](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22853/) - I'm have been given access to read your journal thread; however, I'm not given permission to write a reply to it - perhaps because, I'm only a Silver member and you're a Gold member.

So, if you don't mind, may I response to your light setup and times spent which you posted here...

Notes on the positive: I like how you combine the red, infrared with your UVB and that you rotate every 5 minutes.

May I suggest you take your equipment outside during the cooler fall & spring and colder winter time and blow a fan over your moist exposed skin during your sessions. Please consider moving our UVB lighting off the floor to torso height.

Also please consider increasing you red and infrared time throughout the day.

We know the red and infrared therapy is very important for all mitochondrial functions; however, you may want to consider adding UVA.

I use [https://www.amazon.com/Mega-Ray-Mercury-Vapor-Bulb-Watts/dp/B00TQG6DRI/ref=sr\_1\_18?crid=3FXJLAN4ZQ1S](https://www.amazon.com/Mega-Ray-Mercury-Vapor-Bulb-Watts/dp/B00TQG6DRI/ref=sr_1_18?crid=3FXJLAN4ZQ1S) which is a full spectrum UV light.

We know full spectrum UV is what our POMC uses - our Leptin-Melanocortin System - Interactions Between the Melanocortin System and Leptin in Control of Sympathetic Nerve Traffic - [https://www.ahajournals.org/doi/full/10.1161/01.HYP.33.1.542](https://www.ahajournals.org/doi/full/10.1161/01.HYP.33.1.542) The hypothalamic melanocortin system is activated by the melanocortin receptors. The hypothalamic melanocortin system can increase sympathetic nerve traffic when stimulated thermogenically <- _**Cold Therapy**_

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Melanocortin peptides (ACTH, α-MSH, β-MSH, and γ-MSH) are all derived from the common precursor protein [_pro-opiomelanocortin (POMC)_](https://www.nature.com/articles/srep14579).

-   ACTH is a peptide hormone that regulates the secretion of glucocorticoids from the adrenal cortex. This links to disorders linked to adrenal collapse like CFS/ME and Lyme and mold disease as well as electromagnetic hypersensitivity.
-   αlpha-Melanocyte-stimulating hormone (a-MSH) is cleaved from [_pro-opiomelanocortin (POMC)_](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/neuroscience/proopiomelanocortin) in the pituitary. Note: Cell pigmentation is therefore regulated by locally produced alpha-MSH rather than that of pituitary origin.
-   αlpha-MSH is produced by neurons in the ventromedial nucleus has important roles in the regulation of appetite (POMC neuron stimulation results in satiety.) and sexual behavior, while α-MSH secreted from the intermediate lobe of the pituitary regulates the movement of melanin produced from melanocytes. This hormone is linked to infertility, and gender identity issues.
-   [alpha-MSH has potent protective and antiinflammatory effects](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/18612139). At the molecular level, alpha-MSH affects various pathways implicated in regulation of inflammation and protection, i.e., nuclear factor-kappaB activation, expression of adhesion molecules and chemokine receptors, production of proinflammatory cytokines and mediators, IL-10 synthesis, T cell proliferation and activity, inflammatory cell migration, expression of antioxidative enzymes, and apoptosis.
-   β-Endorphin and \[Met\]enkephalin are endogenous opioid peptides with widespread actions in the brain. This is where the current opiate crisis came from.

The five [_melanocortin receptors_](https://www.alomone.com/article/melanocortin-receptors-kidney-nervous-system) (MCRs, [MC1R](https://medlineplus.gov/genetics/gene/mc1r/)∼MC5R) are expressed in a diverse array of tissues and belong to the guanine nucleotide-binding protein-coupled receptor (GPCR) family. MCRs have different affinities to agonists and antagonists causing a vast array of physiological effects. These five melanocortin receptors are members of the seven-transmembrane domain, G-protein coupled receptor (GPCR) superfamily. Melanocortins, the ligands of the receptors are a group of structurally similar peptides consisting of α-, β- and γ-melanocyte stimulating hormone (α, β, γ-MSH) and the adrenocorticotropic hormone (ACTH) all of which are derived from the post-translational processing of a common precursor peptide, [_proopiomelanocortin_](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2253185/) (POMC).

Melanocortin peptide, the steroidogenic melanocortin peptide [_adrenocorticotropic hormone_](https://www.yourhormones.info/hormones/melanocyte-stimulating-hormone/) (ACTH), protects internal organs through at least two mechanisms:

(1) stimulating the production of [_corticosteroids_](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4662771/); and
(2) activating MCRs expression.

The POMC precursor polypeptide is processed into several bioactive products, including α-melanocyte-stimulating hormone (α-MSH), adrenocorticotropic hormone (ACTH), and β-endorphin. These all link to important systems. After secretion, α-MSH binds to the melanocortin 1 receptor (MC1R) located on melanocytes and activates melanin production, and is tied to the leptin-melanocortin system.

The [_melanocortin system_](https://journals.physiology.org/doi/full/10.1152/ajpendo.00434.2002):

_1_) the melanocortin peptides α-, β-, and γ-melanocyte-stimulating hormone (α-, β-, γ-MSH) and adrenocorticotropic hormone (ACTH),
_2_) a family of five seven-transmembrane G protein-coupled melanocortin receptors, and
_3_) the endogenous melanocortin antagonists agouti and agouti-related protein (AGRP).

The production of melanin is regulated by α-melanocyte-stimulating hormone (α-MSH), which is produced from proopiomelanocortin (POMC).

Since you have access to cooler and colder weather, may I suggest you use your red, infrared system with full spectrum UV lighting in the _cold_ while you train, exercise, lift, full functional movement, etc. This way you stimulate your leptin hormones (with cold) as well as building up your melanin stores (the light).

Question: Do you have access to my journal? - [https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/granpa-johns-optimal-journal.23952/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/granpa-johns-optimal-journal.23952/) - feel free to enter the dialog.

Looking Forward...
John

### Sue-UK — 2023-05-07T15:06:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1900-links-for-pbm-studies.26673/post-321045>

> [Daulatwant said:](/goto/post?id=320767)
>
> For eye related problems there is nothing to stop the degradation or reverse the dammage. I mentioned cerule because macular degeneration was mentioned here, and I provided an anecdote of someone claiming their degeneration was stopped and eyesight even improved while taking cerule.

I don't have a problem with Cerule, but he was taking 16 capsules a day IIRC so at £49 per bottle of 60, it limits it to those who can afford it. The main ingredient is a cyanobacteria, then fucoidin from brown seaweed and the patented product mesenkine is derived from spirulina, so theoretically it could be done another way.

I think there's been a lot more research on spirulina rather than on the klamath strain because of its availability and its potential uses in space.
[https://www.mdpi.com/1660-3397/20/5/299](https://www.mdpi.com/1660-3397/20/5/299).

I'm interested in the light aspect.

I don't have the book ([https://www.sciencedirect.com/book/9780128094341/introduction-to-the-chemistry-of-food](https://www.sciencedirect.com/book/9780128094341/introduction-to-the-chemistry-of-food)) but an extract online

_"Phycocyanin is a pigment-protein complex that biologically functions cooperatively with chlorophyll in photosynthesis. Specifically, phycocyanin increases the efficiency of chlorophyll's oxygen production under low light conditions. Phycocyanins are blue in color which results from their absorption of red-orange wavelengths of light. They are found in [cyanobacteria](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/agricultural-and-biological-sciences/cyanobacteria), a type of blue-green algae, that derives its energy from photosynthesis."_

Chlorophyll has already been associated with capturing photons and generating ATP in mammals

### [Light-harvesting chlorophyll pigments enable mammalian mitochondria to capture photonic energy and produce ATP](https://journals.biologists.com/jcs/article/127/2/388/54610/Light-harvesting-chlorophyll-pigments-enable)

ABSTRACT. Sunlight is the most abundant energy source on this planet. However, the ability to convert sunlight into biological energy in the form of adenosine-5′-triphosphate (ATP) is thought to be limited to chlorophyll-containing chloroplasts in photosynthetic organisms. Here we show that...

 journals.biologists.com

So my questions are how does phycocyanin translate into human photosynthesis, and is it nature's methylene blue, even if the mechanism of action is different?  For example cisplatin toxicity. Jack posted a link to cisplatin toxicity and this paper ([https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads...-to-know-about-ddw-and-bhb.23265/#post-264892](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-have-cancer-and-chemo-you-might-want-to-know-about-ddw-and-bhb.23265/#post-264892))

### [β-Hydroxybutyrate, a ketone body, reduces the cytotoxic effect of cisplatin via activation of HDAC5 in human renal cortical epithelial cells](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0024320519301584)

β-Hydroxybutyrate (βOHB) is a metabolic intermediate that constitutes about 70% of ketone bodies produced in liver from oxidation of fatty acids relea…

 www.sciencedirect.com

Then there's these on cisplatin and MB and spirulina and one on phycocyanin and the gut (although doesn't touch on the changes to light emission from the microbiome).

### [Evaluation of protective effects of methylene blue on cisplatin-induced nephrotoxicity](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0753332222004127)

Cisplatin (CP) is used to treat various types of cancer. However, its usage is limited due to nephrotoxicity. This study aims to examine the applicabi…

 www.sciencedirect.com

### [The protective impacts of Spirulina platensis against cisplatin-induced renal injury through the regulation of oxidative stress, pro-inflammatory cytokines and Bax/Bcl2](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8882809/)

One of the main antineoplastic chemotherapy medications is cisplatin, of which nephropathy is a major side effect. In this current study, we aim to investigate the molecular protective effect of Spirulina platensis (SP) on cisplatin-induced nephrotoxicity. ...

 www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

### [C-phycocyanin alleviated cisplatin-induced oxidative stress and inflammation via gut microbiota—metabolites axis in mice](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9549462/)

C-phycocyanin is a natural protein extracted from Spirulina platensis. We aim to investigate the preventive effect of C-phycocyanin on cisplatin chemotherapy-induced oxidative damage and inflammation. The result showed that C-phycocyanin treatment reduced ...

 www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

Although the experiments were done under artificial lights this gives a clue about spirulina's relationship to light. [https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0005272812000084](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0005272812000084)
I've hacked it using natural sunlight and have noticed benefits, but it would be interesting to know a PBM result ....

I'd also be interested to know the absorption spectra of mesenkine in cerule ....

Thanks for all you do  I use 2 pieces of Sperti D (UVB) and 1 piece of Fiji tanning lamp (UVA), I guess you haven't seen the video I attached: [https://vimeo.com/820403612?share=copy](https://vimeo.com/820403612?share=copy) The FireHawk lamp takes up a lot of space, and it is not easy to find space for more lamps in addition. Sometimes I move the UVA up to body height and remove one of the Sperti D lamps. But I prioritize vitamin D in this setup. And the Sperti D lamps have a lot of flickering and I notice that these must not be exaggerated. I experience too much of this as negative. This rotation of 5 minutes with UVB lamps gives me no negative consequences, at least none that I notice.......

I also get UVA and IR outdoors when I exercise or am outside. Sometimes I just stand in front of the FireHawk and enjoy myself, often when I want an extra boost due to things going on in life. Moving the FireHawk lamp is not an easy option... just providing sufficient electricity supply is a challenge. 3500 watts. It is not clear from the picture that the room with the FireHawk lamp is cold in winter and that I can open two of the walls completely. Thanks for your input, because I have some thoughts about improvements that I might now be motivated to implement. I get more red light than this, but break this up in other ways. Among other things, I am outdoors a lot. Today I sat out on the terrace, in the sun with extra light from the Fiji lamp. I will post two other options on my profile.

> Lets see how I feel about it in a year....

Hi Inger

Re the black beans, there's quite an interesting bit on black beans in William Li's book Eat to Beat Disease about white haricot beans and black beans. In a mouse experiment, after 3 weeks they found that mice eating beans had a 71 fold increase in a healthy bacteria provotella which makes antiflammatory SCFA's (acetate, proprionate, butyrate) and a 2.3 fold increase in ruminococcus which breaks down plant cells as another way to increase SCFAs. The bean eaters also had an 81 per cent decrease in the harmful bacteria that break down the protective mucus in the gut. They also found that the protective mucus secreting cells of the upper colon increased by 60 per cent in the white bean eaters and 120 per cent in the mice fed black beans. There's no explanation of why the difference, but black beans would be a source of melanin ......so I'm wondering if its related to the light emitted by the microbiome....

The bean eaters also had an 81 per cent decrease in the harmful bacteria that break down the protective mucus in the gut. There's no explanation of why the difference, but black beans would be a source of melanin ......so I'm wondering if its related to the light emitted by the microbiome....

That is very interesting Sue. Thanks for sharing.
I have a feeling these beans, especially black beans, could be a really cool thing, but lets see what I think after a couple more months into the experiment  Beans are also high in B1 and many other nutrients.... and I really like what they do for the gut microbiome!

### John Schumacher — 2023-06-27T03:09:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1900-links-for-pbm-studies.26673/post-322922>

Transcranial near-infrared light in treatment of neurodegenerative diseases
[https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fphar.2022.965788/full](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fphar.2022.965788/full)

We know there is a pathophysiological role of the glymphatic and brain lymphatics system in treating neurodegenerative diseases with NIR light stimulations.

But there is new evidence showing that the potential pathway is the nuclear factor-erythroid 2 like 2 (Nrf2) mechanism.

Photobiomodulation Controls Keratinocytes Inflammatory Response through Nrf2 and Reduces Langerhans Cells Activation
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10045240/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10045240/)

Since previous studies showed that nuclear factor-erythroid 2 like 2 (Nrf2) is a master regulator of the skin inflammatory response, we have addressed its role in controlling inflammation by PBM. Primary human keratinocytes (KCs) stimulated with 2,4-dinitrochlorobenzene (DNCB) to mimic pro-inflammatory stress were illuminated with two wavelengths: 660 nm or 520 nm. Both lights significantly reduced the mRNA expression of the DNCB-triggered _TNF-α_, _IL-6_, and _IL-8_ cytokines in KCs, while they enhanced Nrf2 pathway activation. PBM-induced Nrf2 is a key regulator of the inflammatory response in KCs since its absence abolished the regulatory effect of light on cytokines production.

The outermost layer of the skin, mainly composed of keratinocytes (KCs), some melanocytes, and immune cells such as resident memory T cells and Langerhans cells (LCs). Long considered passive components of the cutaneous physical barrier, KCs are now described as sentinel cells actively contributing to the immunological barrier of the skin \[[2](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10045240/#B2-antioxidants-12-00766),[3](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10045240/#B3-antioxidants-12-00766),[4](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10045240/#B4-antioxidants-12-00766)\]. KCs play a central role in innate immunity as sensors of danger signals or stress factors through different types of Pattern Recognition Receptors (PRRs), such as Toll-like receptors (TLRs) \[[2](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10045240/#B2-antioxidants-12-00766)\]. The recognition of the cognitive ligands by PRRs on KCs triggers downstream signaling cascades, such as the nuclear factor kappa B (NF-κB) pathway leading to the production of pro-inflammatory mediators such as Tumor necrosis factor-alpha (TNF-α), Interleukin (IL)-1, IL-6, and IL-8. These cytokines and chemokines orchestrate the early immune response of KCs to pathogens and chemical challenges by activating residing immune cells in the skin and recruiting leukocytes to cutaneous sites of inflammation KCs. Therefore, KCs are immunocompetent effector cells that can initiate adequate immune responses to maintain skin homeostasis and defenses and thus have a crucial role in regulating skin inflammation \[[5](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10045240/#B5-antioxidants-12-00766)\].

The nuclear factor erythroid-2-related factor 2 (Nrf2) pathway \[[6](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10045240/#B6-antioxidants-12-00766)\] is well established that it regulates the gene expression of a broad spectrum of antioxidant enzymes, such as superoxide dismutase (SOD) and glutamate-cysteine ligase catalytic subunit (GCLC), cytoprotective enzymes such as heme oxygenase 1 (HO-1), as well as phase II drug detoxification enzymes such as NAD(P)H quinone oxidoreductase 1 (NQO1) \[[10](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10045240/#B10-antioxidants-12-00766),[11](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10045240/#B11-antioxidants-12-00766)\]. Exposure to oxidative or electrophilic stress leads to the dissociation of Nrf2 from its cytoplasmic inhibitor Kelch-like ECH-associated protein 1 (Keap 1). Nrf2 then translocates into the nucleus and binds to the antioxidant-responsive elements (ARE) of target gene promoters, initiating the transcription of a battery of antioxidants and cytoprotective genes, as cited before \[[11](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10045240/#B11-antioxidants-12-00766),[12](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10045240/#B12-antioxidants-12-00766)\]. In addition to the primary function of Nrf2 as a master regulator of xenobiotic detoxification and redox homeostasis within the cells, recent investigations revealed that Nrf2 also plays an essential role in controlling the inflammatory response \[[13](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10045240/#B13-antioxidants-12-00766),[14](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10045240/#B14-antioxidants-12-00766),[15](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10045240/#B15-antioxidants-12-00766)\]; in particular, skin inflammation \[[16](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10045240/#B16-antioxidants-12-00766),[17](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10045240/#B17-antioxidants-12-00766)\].

Nrf2 can control inflammation directly and indirectly \[[20](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10045240/#B20-antioxidants-12-00766),[21](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10045240/#B21-antioxidants-12-00766)\]. Indirect mechanisms to counteract inflammation involve ROS and RNS modulation by Nrf2 and crosstalk of the Nrf2 pathway with redox-sensitive key inflammatory pathways, including NF-κB \[[21](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10045240/#B21-antioxidants-12-00766)\]. Recently, it has been described that Nrf2 can also directly inhibit the transcription of pro-inflammatory cytokine genes such as _IL-6_ and _IL-1β_ through direct DNA binding close to these genes, independently from ARE, contrary to the widely accepted view that Nrf2 inhibits inflammation through redox control \[[22](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10045240/#B22-antioxidants-12-00766)\].

PBM has been shown to modulate the activity of the NF-κB and mitogen-activated protein kinase (MAPK) pathways, both involved in the regulation of cytokine production and other inflammatory processes \[[29](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10045240/#B29-antioxidants-12-00766),[32](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10045240/#B32-antioxidants-12-00766),[33](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10045240/#B33-antioxidants-12-00766)\]. We investigate PBM’s anti-inflammatory response using a two-dimensional model of primary human KCs challenged with DNCB, a contact sensitizer used to mimic pro-inflammatory stress. Herein, we showed that Nrf2 is a crucial factor controlling DNCB-induced pro-inflammatory cytokines production in KCs upon light exposure.

We showed an activation of the Nrf2 signaling by red and green lights in DNCB-stimulated KCs. Indeed, 660 nm and 520 nm LEDs further enhanced Nrf2 accumulation and nuclear translocation compared to the DNCB-treated group.

We showed that the exposure of skin explants to red light (660 nm) prevented the DNCB-induced LCs modifications and migration. It also decreased the expression of TNF-α in the epidermis and tended to regulate the level of some pro-inflammatory cytokine secretion. Thus, this result provides evidence for the immunomodulatory effect of PBM that might contribute to its anti-inflammatory activity preventing allergen sensitization in atopic or allergic dermatitis.

In summary, the data presented here support previous studies on the anti-inflammatory effect of PBM. Our findings provide new evidence that the PBM’s beneficial activity in human KCs with an inflammatory response is at least dependent on the Nrf2 pathway. Furthermore, the therapeutic effect of PBM in response to DNCB does not seem to involve regulation of the level of H2O2 in KCs, but rather a modulation of the skin immune response, probably through the regulation of innate cytokines that are crucial for initiating and perpetuating inflammation. PBM controls TNF-α expression in the epidermis of human explants and prevented the activation of LCs and their migration into the dermis in response to chemical stress.

Transcranial near-infrared laser improves postoperative neurocognitive disorder via SIRT3/AMPK/Nrf2 pathway
[https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fnins.2022.1100915/full](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fnins.2022.1100915/full)
Conclusion: Transcranial near-infrared laser improved cognitive impairment with PND, which may be a promising therapeutic for PND.

Transcranial near-infrared laser has shown positive results in the treatment of stroke, TBI, and neurodegenerative diseases ([Oron et al., 2006](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fnins.2022.1100915/full#B33); [Xuan et al., 2015](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fnins.2022.1100915/full#B53); [Lu et al., 2017](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fnins.2022.1100915/full#B26)). TNIL at a wavelength of 810 nm can effectively and non-invasively penetrate into the skull and brain tissue ([Streeter et al., 2004](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fnins.2022.1100915/full#B42)). It was reported that 810 nm laser improved cognitive function and increased BDNF and synaptogenesis in TBI. Besides, studies showed that the most effective treatment was 3 consecutive days after surgery ([Xuan et al., 2014](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fnins.2022.1100915/full#B54)). Our study showed that TNIL improved spatial learning and memory. The results indicated that TNIL improved cognitive function.

The reason for this hypotheses ->
Recent Advances in the Role of Nuclear Factor Erythroid-2-Related Factor 2 in Spinal Cord Injury: Regulatory Mechanisms and Therapeutic Options
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9226435/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9226435/)

Nrf2 signaling pathway is a key pharmacological target for the treatment of spinal cord injury (SCI), and activating the Nrf2 signaling pathway can effectively treat the inflammatory injury and oxidative stress after SCI.

Enjoy,
John Schumacher

### John Schumacher — 2023-11-22T21:28:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1900-links-for-pbm-studies.26673/post-328547>

Weeklong improved colour contrasts sensitivity after single 670 nm exposures associated with enhanced mitochondrial function
[https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-021-02311-1](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-021-02311-1)

We showed that a single 3 min 670 nm exposure, at much lower energies than previously used (at only 8 mW/cm2), is sufficient to significantly improve for 1 week cone mediated colour contrast thresholds (detection) in ageing populations (37–70 years) to levels associated with younger subjects.

_Time of day of exposure is critical, as 670 nm light is only effective in the morning._

Cold Laser Biophotonic stimulation

_Frequency wobbling_
Specific management of stimulation frequencies based on the dynamic (acceleration or deceleration), with the signal variation used as the basis for the information decoded by the organism.

The Qi expresses this concept of Vital Breath, Energy, and Force in action and represents Life. It symbolises Movement. This energy circulates continuously in the meridians, expressing the Yin and the Yang, in their equilibrium and in their regularly alternating cycle. They enable harmonious functioning of the living being individually incorporated into the immediate environment and in the entire universe.

During the detailed overall clinical examination, after identifying a dysfunction and analysing its causes, the place where the energy is lacking must be tonified, where it is in excess it must be sedated, and energy flows must be harmonised and redirected when the crossover points are disturbed.
Tonification, sedation, harmonisation…, biological information is brought by wobulation

Impulse frequency influences the reaction. If this frequency is set at a fixed level for too long the body no longer responds and becomes indifferent to stimulation.

Stimulation is effective if it is dynamic and varies in the same way as the Qi moves perpetually through the network of meridians.

In the same way as the Qi rises and then falls (for example: in each meridian), the wobulated stimulation increases or reduces the number of impulses emitted per second in a complex mathematical progression.

Example frequency wobbles:
· **Analgesic** (E and G frequencies) : reduces pain transmission to the central nervous system and improves local trophicity.
· **Regeneration** (A, B and F frequencies) : accelerates tissue reconstruction, restores cell functions and accelerates the healing and regeneration, process both superficially and deep.
· **Relaxation** (C, D and G frequencies) : facilitates release of tensions and muscle contractures.
· **Nogier frequency\*\* scanning** (A to G frequencies): stimulation of pathological areas regardless of the origin of these.
· **Individual frequency selection** : Each of the 7 [**Nogier frequencies**](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.sedatelec.com%2Fen%2Fnogier-frequencies%2F%3Ffbclid%3DIwAR0TLVfFU1tDD5kB7iTVrTyCXRzwEc4MYQ1KkcODz5vBw3dNGnfkWONzTrs&h=AT3aXkf4wM4aZXu66m72t_VcBt1iRCJQKRWJHcd1ou6db2MkrF59HuzuWmiXSAVKX6qCMtCT7Qrxy_kF4GAM9rOZOxqO6J3aSpwXX7cE9MM-O-sn3yR5XEN3nX5DPWgkQ-5D&__tn__=-UK-R&c[0]=AT0zcPd9Bgpwv7Lt5RiB783GGxGomBOWdpaB809uLS8lPRimr7uSNZnK2jvz4SPaWErFoWf4LqP6eMed0F0ALgnXNeCjn1a7KQhps4aOxkZwbNe685LYkGrh7YBSKui3Un4pA12q67MVj2fPWMKn-UEbA3tywVsYZxaThBPRuNjA2yi1739Ar3u9dITJ0IPINaR6f3KLez9nnPmnrbgex_A)\*\* can be selected individually to act on a specific physiological function.

### John Schumacher — 2023-11-23T06:55:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/1900-links-for-pbm-studies.26673/post-328564>

For those of you on this form who have the money to spend, check out the [https://huelightusa.com/product/whole-body-photobiomodulation-pbm-chamber/](https://huelightusa.com/product/whole-body-photobiomodulation-pbm-chamber/)

It uses the Nogier Frequenices over the waveforms 530 ~ 940nm at cm2 /70mW requiring a dedicated 220V 30 Amp 3Wire with 30 Amp and disconnect switch

Photobiomodulation for Traumatic Brain Injury and Stroke
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5803455/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5803455/)

Light passes through the scalp and skull, where depending on the wavelength it is absorbed by two different chromophores. Red and NIR (up to 940nm) is primarily absorbed by cytochrome c oxidase in the mitochondrial respiratory chain of the cortical neurons. Longer wavelength NIR light (980nm, 1064nm) is primarily absorbed by heat and light-sensitive transient receptor potential ion channels. In both cases cell signaling and messenger molecules are upregulated as a result of stimulated mitochondrial activity, including reactive oxygen species (ROS), and adenosine triphosphate (ATP). hv is light, TRPV is transient receptor potential vanilloid (ion channels).

Penetration of 670 nm and 810 nm light into the brain when delivered by a transcranial or a transphenoidal approach, and found that the best combination was 810 nm delivered transphenoidally ([Pitzschke et al. 2015](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5803455/#R71)). Yaroslavsky et al. examined light penetration of different wavelengths through different parts of the brain tissue (white brain matter, gray brain matter, cerebellum, and brainstem tissues, pons, thalamus). _Best penetration was found with wavelengths between 1000 and 1100 nm_ ([Yaroslavsky et al. 2002](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5803455/#R91)). Henderson and Morries found that between 0.45% and 2.90% of 810 nm or 980 nm light penetrated through 3 cm of scalp, skull and brain tissue in ex vivo lamb heads ([Henderson and Morries 2015a](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5803455/#R28)).

Ok, its been a few years now. Product development, testing, clinical trials are completed. The company name & product(s) is: [https://www.lumithera.com/products/](https://www.lumithera.com/products/)

SEATTLE, Sept. 27, 2023–(BUSINESS WIRE)–LumiThera Inc., a medical device company commercializing a photobiomodulation (PBM) treatment for ocular disorders and disease, today announced it is a recipient of a small business innovative research (SBIR) phase II grant from the National Institute of Health (NIH) and the division of the National Eye Institute (NEI) of up to $2.3M in funding over two years.

The studies:
Thirty subjects (46 eyes) were treated with the _Valeda Light Delivery System_, wherein subjects underwent two series of treatments (3· per week for 3–4 weeks) over 1 year. Approximately 50% of PBM-treated subjects showed improvement of 5 letters versus 13.6% in sham-treated subjects at M1. High responding subjects (5-letter improvement) in the PBM-treated group showed a gain of 8 letters after initial treatment (P , 0.01) and exhibited earlier stages of age-related macular degeneration disease.
[https://www.lumithera.com/wp-content/uploads/A\_DOUBLE\_MASKED\_RANDOMIZED\_SHAM\_CONTROLLED.96062.pdf](https://www.lumithera.com/wp-content/uploads/A_DOUBLE_MASKED_RANDOMIZED_SHAM_CONTROLLED.96062.pdf)

[https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s40123-022-00640-6](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s40123-022-00640-6)

### Jack Kruse — 2021-12-03T04:40:59+0200

Jack is perfectly positioned to weave Bitcoin into popular culture.

**He understands digital energy.** He is one of the first Bitcoin maxi's and refuses to engage with anything but Bitcoin.

**He understands social media.** From Twitter to weaving in influencer marketing to Cash App to his personal philanthropic efforts.

**He understands money.** From Square and Cash app he intimately understands the evolution of user experience and money.

**He understands culture.** Jack is a celebrity. Both in silicon valley and in the mainstream. He rubs shoulders with some of the most influential people in the world.

**He understands PoW > PoS** \= Energy gives currency value, not the other way around. This is the basis of why PoW > PoS. You can print money but you can't print molecules. All currency MUST be denominated in energy units when you boil down your capital energy cost = the EU is in deep trouble.

Dorsey was in Santa Teresa Costa Rica when i was there, never met him personally but tonne of Bitcoiners there had hung with him.

Heard he's a good dude and was right in firing line past year. His exit strategy was quiet and didn't try to put blame anywhere else. Just said "I'll prove myself with my actions".

Instead of spending time apologising he simply moved on and put his attention where he believes it works well... Blockchain technology and freedom.

I respect that.

There's many in the tech industry that I believe are stuck in these positions and have to navigate threats from both sides. I've long thought Elon may be another.

The cabal will murder your family and completely deface you, or even murder you entirely.

But the conspiracy theorists will also hang you out to dry and say you're controlled Opp based on no evidence except association.

We have to wear their shoes to understand their lives. I've never been a CEO of twitter, but I bet it's fucked.

He left. That's good enough for me

# Thread: multi sig.....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/multi-sig.26675/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2021-12-03T05:05:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/multi-sig.26675/post-307007>

Does anyone have any experience with multi sig?

I have been thinking about this for months but just starting to look into it now.

Jack mentioned unchained capital and NYDG on the pow wow.

It has worried me for a long time - what if we were both killed in a car accident? What if we both get dementia?

When leaving the country - could customs confiscate our ledger? If we put it in checked baggage - it could be stolen.

Thoughts anyone?

### Richard Watson — 2021-12-03T06:55:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/multi-sig.26675/post-307011>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=307007)
>
> could customs confiscate our ledger?

At least the are small enough to be hidden, but then like an efpos card, they are useless without the code anyway.

# Thread: Favorite water filters? RO water + spring?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favorite-water-filters-ro-water-spring.26676/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Tfreeman — 2021-12-03T18:09:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favorite-water-filters-ro-water-spring.26676/post-307016>

Hi!

What are everyone's favorites RO filters, countertop versions, and spring water dispensers? If there is another threat dedicated to this, I'd love the link!

### John Schumacher — 2021-12-03T18:17:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favorite-water-filters-ro-water-spring.26676/post-307017>

You may want to consider testing drinking water no matter where it comes from. Example

[https://www.pacelabs.com/environmen...s/specialty-services/drinking-water-analysis/](https://www.pacelabs.com/environmental-sciences/testing-services/specialty-services/drinking-water-analysis/)
[https://www.pacelabs.com/environmental-sciences/testing-services/specialty-services/ezherbicide/](https://www.pacelabs.com/environmental-sciences/testing-services/specialty-services/ezherbicide/)
​

### Tfreeman — 2021-12-03T21:55:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favorite-water-filters-ro-water-spring.26676/post-307027>

This is super thankful. Thanks, John!

### Tfreeman — 2021-12-03T21:56:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favorite-water-filters-ro-water-spring.26676/post-307028>

I typically have mountain valley delivered in 5 gallon bottles and when I'm out I buy reverse osmosis bottled, but I feel there may be better options out there that I will also test.

A fair question: what did water consist of before civilization? Industrialization?

### JanSz — 2021-12-03T22:09:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favorite-water-filters-ro-water-spring.26676/post-307029>

> [Tfreeman said:](/goto/post?id=307028)
>
> I typically have mountain valley delivered in 5 gallon bottles and when I'm out I buy reverse osmosis bottled, but I feel there may be better options out there that I will also test.
>
> A fair question: what did water consist of before civilization? Industrialization?

I tested waters, in my kitchen sink, and the cheapest RO water from Costco.
Costco sells boxes of 40 half liter bottles for under $3.
Both of these waters are about 150ppm D/H

Popular mineral waters have little over 150ppm.
When mineral water is missing minimal amounts of deuterium they make tremendous deal about that, then their price is so large that one is better of just buying straight DDW-25.

....................

### John Schumacher — 2021-12-05T03:06:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/favorite-water-filters-ro-water-spring.26676/post-307078>

> [Tfreeman said:](/goto/post?id=307028)
>
> I typically have mountain valley delivered in 5 gallon bottles and when I'm out I buy reverse osmosis bottled, but I feel there may be better options out there that I will also test.
>
> A fair question: what did water consist of before civilization? Industrialization?

From **My Optimal Journal** (subject) - Water protocol: The drinking water fifty years ago in the foothills of Boulder Colorado was amazing; in Hollywood California – not so good. The first bottled water I started purchasing back then was Deep Rock - a filtered distilled water, bottled in glass. Over the years, I have looked for good palatable water - some successes, some not so. I’m still looking for an affordable “solution”.
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/granpa-johns-optimal-journal.23952/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/granpa-johns-optimal-journal.23952/)

# Thread: Sunrooms? Light from glass windows?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunrooms-light-from-glass-windows.26678/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martha Ray — 2021-12-04T06:09:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunrooms-light-from-glass-windows.26678/post-307040>

Researching and looking at homes and have come across several with **sunrooms**\----
what could be done with with a sunroom...maybe make it into a greenhouse?
**
Am I right that sunrooms are NOT healthy for humans?**

My family loves to sit near the windows of our great room in the winter and I'm thinking that is not healthy.
**Am I right?**

**I also have no ideas about what** glass choices for windows **are best for health. I am pretty sure the great room wall of windows are tinted to protect the furniture. Also in that room two window pannels beside the fireplace need to be replaced and the whole room needs the blinds replaced..we did not know and put unhealthy blinds up year ago before we knew to be careful with materials.**

Realators, home remoldeling and decor sites bill sunrooms as health and a place to sunbathe?

How should we even interact around our home windows?

**Isn't sunlight through glass windows home and cars harmful (unhealthy and possibly carcinogenic?) for humans?

What think you all...?**

### Dan2 — 2021-12-04T08:31:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunrooms-light-from-glass-windows.26678/post-307043>

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/uv-transmitting-plastic-for-windows.24125/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/uv-transmitting-plastic-for-windows.24125/)

### JanSz — 2021-12-04T14:29:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunrooms-light-from-glass-windows.26678/post-307051>

> [Martha Ray said:](/goto/post?id=307040)
>
> **Isn't sunlight through glass windows home and cars harmful (unhealthy and possibly carcinogenic?) for humans?
>
> What think you all...?**

Note that [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) advises keeping the window in the car crack open while driving.
Light goes in thru the crack, bounces off surfaces,
now inside the car you have all the information from the light that you need (if you take the clothes off).

Information from light----->>100% need to stay alive
Energy from (real time) light ---->is minimal, close to nothing (on equator, during noon)(when compared to the energy of matrix water synthesis and its decomposition in the presence of melanin)

\----------------------------
Do the same with the sunroom. Keep some windows crack open. Preferably windows that are directly in the sunlight.
....

# Thread: Environmental Health Consultant coming Indoor air+
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/environmental-health-consultant-coming-indoor-air.26679/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martha Ray — 2021-12-04T06:58:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/environmental-health-consultant-coming-indoor-air.26679/post-307041>

I set up an appointment with an **Environmental Health Consultant** to talk to us about some concerns I have about home health and about his services.

His specialties include:

-   Asbestos-containing Materials
    -   ACM Identification
    -   ACM Management
    -   ACM Abatement
    -   Asbestos Project Design and Monitoring
    -   Asbestos Regulations Consulting
-   Lead-Based Paints
    -   LBP Inspections
    -   LBP Risk Assessments
    -   LBP Regulations
-   Indoor Air Quality Investigations, Testing & Inspections
    -   COVID-19 (coronavirus) testing and air exchange rate measurements
    -   Mold
        -   Mold Inspections
        -   Black Mold Testing
        -   Mold Assessments
        -   Mold Remediation Design and Consulting
    -   Radon
        -   Radon Gas Measurements
        -   Radon Testing for Home and Residential
        -   Radon Inspections for Commercial Properties

I will most likely test the Indoor air the day he comes ($80.00). I ask him if there was anything I should do before he comes and he said to not open the house up or run the air purifiers 24 hours before our appointment.

For the past 12 years we have been using _Austin Air_ brand STANDARD purifiers almost 24 /7. They use charcoal filters. I used to have one of there large high end units that after I researched one of the materials I found it may not good for thyroid health. When we replaced that large unit filter last I went with their plain standard charcoal filter and have been please with them. The small EMF meter I have does not register the units as high but I still also wonder about running them 24/7.

Not sure this consultant is all that holistic but he has a very good reputation in town, he come highly recommended by people I think well of and he does not personally provide any remediation services himself. His fee is $225. for this appointment.

**Have any of you had experenice with _Environmental Health Consultants_ and can you please share your experiences, as well as any** **questions I might consider asking him**....

**I'll report back after I see him....**

### JanSz — 2021-12-04T14:23:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/environmental-health-consultant-coming-indoor-air.26679/post-307050>

Be careful.
You may not be able to sell your home (until after you clean up the mess he is going to write about), and send a copy of it to your town.

.........

# Thread: Going somewhere
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-somewhere.26680/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Liam Taylor — 2021-12-04T15:53:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-somewhere.26680/post-307054>

Going somewhere by Andrew Marino reccomended by Jack repeatedly over the past several years makes a mockery of the scientific process used by many around the world. These same studies are being used to justify extreme interference in the lives of individuals. Having read that book I think it's preposterous that a person would go along with what these fraudsters dictate.

I just want to celebrate that book, thank Jack for recommending it, and to ask anyone else here who has read it to vouch for how prescient and relevant to our current circumstances it is.

### Pablo — 2021-12-04T17:23:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-somewhere.26680/post-307060>

Yeah, it is freaking great, great wake up call on how science has been corrupt for years. IMO solution to this comes from individual responsability, waking the fuck up, always asking questions, not delegating authority, pushing in self improvement as a way to change the world, etc. However, most people get in the path we are at only through some traumatic exprience, and I find it is fucking hard to wake people up to this reality otherwise, since most people are just comfortably numb.

Lets celebrate that book, Andrew Marino, Jack Kruse, and so many other heroes, known and unkown, who had fought for truth, stayed strong under huge pressure, and not succumbed to mediocrity, self-deception, pure selfishness and other lower sentiments in detriment of the human species.

Well said sir, well said.

# Thread: Anyone Moving to Mexico?  El Salvador? Central America?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-moving-to-mexico-el-salvador-central-america.26684/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 38
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2021-12-05T06:05:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-moving-to-mexico-el-salvador-central-america.26684/post-307088>

Can we pool our thoughts, inspiration, intuition, knowledge and sheer determination to make a huge move like this?

Some of you have already made this huge move......

What tips can you share?
What mistakes did you make?
What worked great?
What did you think would matter huge? but, in hindsight, didn't matter at all?

A week ago when we decided we to had to get out of Aus, I kept thinking of all our favourite possessions ....all the things I couldn't possibly leave behind, and live without.

But as the week has progressed all the stuff we have loved and collected over a number of years and has given us a lot of pleasure and we have wonderful memories - doesn't really matter in the scheme of things.

We will always have those memories in our heart and TA DAH ....time to make new memories!

Now, today, none of this matters. A couple of suitcases with some clothes and photos and we are good to go.

Will we regret this? I don't think so - it is all just stuff.

The only important thing is my DH ......wherever we are together will be home and it is time to start a new chapter ......
Jack would say we are long overdo making this decision!

It is so hard to realise that the place we love has turned into a nightmare .....and it will never go back to what it was.

At the beginning of this week we were terrified but knew we had to do this.
Now - we just want to get this done and move on.

I have lots of questions.... but for now...

#1 Do we need to buy health insurance?

### Anne V — 2021-12-05T13:05:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-moving-to-mexico-el-salvador-central-america.26684/post-307093>

Best news of the year

### Pablo — 2021-12-05T14:24:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-moving-to-mexico-el-salvador-central-america.26684/post-307094>

I also began pondering to move from Spain if they put compulsory vax... It is not sure but some politicians are begining to consider it, both nationally and at the EU level. So uncertain times, jesus, but we should have several plans just in case... Nayib Bukele recently tweeted he wouldn't put any passport, masks or compulsory vacination in El Salvador, so it is pretty damm attractive place, and there is good Sun and beach... sounds cool.

We need a place:'
\- Non tiranical
\-Good for redox
\- With job opportunities, at least for broke people like me, not so much for people with decent earnings

I apreciate any suggestions

### Marko Pollo — 2021-12-05T17:44:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-moving-to-mexico-el-salvador-central-america.26684/post-307097>

### Marko Pollo — 2021-12-06T17:37:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-moving-to-mexico-el-salvador-central-america.26684/post-307175>

I was in South America for 6 months at the start of Covid, three months longer than planned. I had some business documents to sign in the states, but couldn't return or use the American Consulate to have the docs notarized. Prior to trip I thought I could always just return for a day or two to handle business, but covid has changed this. For this reason I highly recommend leaving a **signed limited power of attorney** with someone you trust highly in your home country. I have used docusign and other apps before but you cannot get a doc notarized if you are not physically in state where notary is licensed.

### Jack Kruse — 2021-12-06T22:37:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anyone-moving-to-mexico-el-salvador-central-america.26684/post-307190>

^^^I did this after I sold Real Estate while living abroad 3 months last year.

### Jack Kruse — 2021-12-05T23:17:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-gates.26687/post-307117>

In 2010, Gates gave a TED Talk where he suggested that “if we do a really great job on new vaccines, health care, reproductive health services, we could lower the global population by 10 or 15 percent. . .”

His apparent suggestion that vaccines could help reduce the population raised suspicions.

Defenders of Gates argued that his statement was poorly worded and the role of vaccines would be to reduce infant mortality and thereby convince poor families that they need not have so many children.

However, one incident calls this into question. It involves the Gates-founded & funded GAVI, originally the “Global Alliance for Vaccines and Immunizations,” later rebranded as “Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance.” GAVI was working with the WHO and UNICEF (the United Nations Children's Fund) to distribute tetanus vaccines to women in Ghana. In 2014, the Kenya Conference of Catholic Bishops accused them of running a secret sterilization campaign against these women. The tetanus shots, usually given every ten years to everyone, were administered only to women of childbearing age, a total of five times, one shot every six months.

The bishops later subjected the shots to chemical analysis showing they were contaminated with human chorionic gonadotropin (hCG). The WHO and the Rockefeller Foundation had been researching the use of tetanus toxoid hCG combinations to produce “sterilizing vaccines” as early as 1972. According to the analysis of the bishops, these women seemed to be dosed with such sterilizing vaccines without their knowledge or consent.

[https://www.corbettreport.com/mp4/whoisbillgates.mp4](https://www.corbettreport.com/mp4/whoisbillgates.mp4)

### Jack Kruse — 2021-12-05T23:22:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-gates.26687/post-307118>

Mandates harm and offer no liability. Do Not Comply. **[https://twitter.com/Virg21Nicholls/status/1467234764360851459](https://twitter.com/Virg21Nicholls/status/1467234764360851459)**

Liability shields protect and incentivize the distribution of unsafe products, but from the pharmaceutical industry's perspective, they protect their profits from being eaten away by lawsuits.

Within the United States, total liability for vaccines is provided by the 1986 National Childhood Vaccine Injury Act to any vaccine added to the CDC schedule for children or pregnant women. Once on the schedule for children or pregnant women, the liability applies to all Americans, even adult men.

Liability is also given for drugs and vaccines developed during pandemics declared by the Public Readiness and Emergency Preparedness (PREP) Act of 2005, so long as the emergency declared has no existing treatments.

These liability shields are not in any way analogous to insurance underwriting. They are meant to provide liability for products that insurance companies would never agree to underwrite. In fact, the 1986 Act was a response to the insurance markets for vaccines collapsing after a 1983 study finding that the DTP vaccine was killing one in every 300 babies that received it.

While these acts protect US pharmaceutical companies from being sued by Americans, they don't guarantee liability protections for their products distributed abroad. This is why the premier of NSW had to be paid off to give Pfizer protection. She was forced to quit but not much has changed in Oz.

They remain the largest most recent new fascist country on Earth.

# Thread: Journal for Anna Vargo - Sleep, Irritable Bladder, Sinus, Headaches
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-anna-vargo-sleep-irritable-bladder-sinus-headaches.26689/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 93
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Anna Vargo — 2021-12-06T21:10:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-anna-vargo-sleep-irritable-bladder-sinus-headaches.26689/post-307185>

Hello! I am excited to be here. I just started following this forum Nov. 2021. Little late to the game but better late than never! The information from Dr. Kruse and people willing to help is simply amazing. I hope to learn a lot and hopefully help someone along the way.

In 2016 this journey of healing began for me. I went to my husband and said “I know you think I am already a health nut but I am not doing something right. I should be feeling great and I feel worse every day”. Turns out I was doing everything wrong. I took classes on Integrative Nutrition and became certified in Integrative Nutrition Coaching only to find out everything I was taught was dead wrong.

The first time I saw healing was in late 2016 when I started eating Low Carb/High Fat. I have been on different phases of a low carb/ keto/keto carnivore WOE for 5 years and my main goal has been to prevent Diabetes and Alzheimer's both which are very prevalent in my family history. Mom suffered with Alzheimer’s since mid 60’s and passed away in her 70’s. What is even worse is three of her sisters went down the same path. Thankfully, one aunt is still alive and sharp. My dad and his family all had a form of diabetes. Dad died of a stroke due to diabetes at 59. I have experienced many improvements from a LCHF WOE (below) but looking to further optimize and discover why I still have some unresolved issues.

I just started down this Quantum path (sunrise, sunshine, Leptin RX, grounding,etc.) about 3 months ago so I suspect there is a LOT more good stuff to come.

Issues Resolved or much improved:
Pre Diabetes
Hypothyroidism
Hormone Imbalance
IBS C
Low Energy
Constant Hunger
Peripheral Neuropathy
Heavy metals - used to have high mercury, infrared sauna resolved

Resolved with LCHF WOE:
Energy returned after 2 years Low Carb
No longer Pre Diabetic
Thyroid and Hormones no longer tanked with BHRT (2 years)
Peripheral Neuropathy in feet and ankles 99% gone
Spider veins disappearing
Constipation completely resolved
Hunger mostly improved but still crave sweets and creamy fats
Bloating and gas improving recently

Unresolved Issues:
No longer pre diabetic but fasting BS is still high 90's
Irregular Sleep (wake up 1-2 X a night even with 3 months of light therapy)
Irritable Bladder - excessive urination especially in the morning, some incontinence
Persistent Sinus Issues (nose always running)
Can’t lose last 15 pounds
Headaches come and go - seem to be related to sinus issues
Hair loss has slowed down but still thin
Very low leptin (.8 non-fasted)

I have very low leptin ( .8 non fasted) and trying to find out why and working on the Leptin RX. I am having a hard time eating before 10 AM. I almost feel nauseous to eat before 10 am. I am working on it. BHRT about two years ago, sunrise, sunset now for 3 months. Huge attitude and energy boost . Grounding for 30 minutes a day for a week. I am taking cold showers, building up to cold plunges. My wish list: better hormone status, better sleep, sinus issues resolved, would love to lose 12 pounds & of course Alzheimer's prevention.

Also, I just learned I am haplotype j1d. Look forward to learning more about what this means in terms of light, magnetism and water. My Mom and grandmother was at latitude 37.81. I am currently at 40.28 in Ohio.

Nice to meet you [@Anna Vargo](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/25875/)
Congrads on all your improvements.
If you don't mine may I jump to Unresolved Issues:

No longer pre diabetic but fasting BS is still high 90's

This typically means its a bigger problem than just a metabolic "diet" issue; but as Dr [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) says - It has more to do with your "redox potential"

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/what-is-redox-potential-fundamentally.22730/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/what-is-redox-potential-fundamentally.22730/)​

Irregular Sleep (wake up 1-2 X a night even with 3 months of light therapy)

This typically means your circadian clock is off

[https://jackkruse.com/time-17-melatonin-insulin-solar-metronomes/](https://jackkruse.com/time-17-melatonin-insulin-solar-metronomes/)​

Irritable Bladder - excessive urination especially in the morning, some incontinence

This typically can be addressed with light therapy

Transvaginal Photobiomodulation for the Treatment of Chronic Pelvic Pain: A Pilot Study
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8617585/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8617585/)
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...t-a-disease-damn-it.25242/page-11#post-299191](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/aging-is-not-a-disease-damn-it.25242/page-11#post-299191)
​

Persistent Sinus Issues (nose always running)

This typically is a Histamine issue

[https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/3279939/](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/3279939/)
[https://www.pharmaca.com/enzymedica-mucostop-48-capsules](https://www.pharmaca.com/enzymedica-mucostop-48-capsules)
​

Can’t lose last 15 pounds

Typically this has not to do with how much exercise you do

However, strength exercise is very important

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/is-osteoporosis-reversible.26587/#post-306449](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/is-osteoporosis-reversible.26587/#post-306449)​

It is more an indicator of your hormonal metabolism not just leptin, insulin, but the hormones of your adrenals, thyroid, and **pituitary.**
How do you fix that you may ask?

When our [mitochondria](https://www.nature.com/articles/srep12029) misfire and the voltage potential moves from -200 mV to zero, the cell's voltage flips -> this is the beginning of disease. [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3634644/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3634644/)

In order to heal and repair, our cells’ voltage needs to be greater (negatively speaking) than -50mV. Some doctors say, -200mV. So what's this negative all about, I thought positive is good and negative is bad. <- Nope

When a cell begins its downward spiral, the voltage will move from negative to positive. Once this value passes zero, _[the polarity flips](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3713347/)_. You move from health to disease. So, when your cell's clocking in at or above _[+50mV, it's in cancer](https://cancerci.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s12935-016-0348-8)_ territory.

There three elements for increasing our cellular "Energy" quotient
· Photoelectric force
· Biochemical-electrical force / signaling
· Piezoelectric force

You know by now our photoelectric force comes from <- our sun. Our cells when they are built on the “correct” ingredients are able absorb photons from the sun and convert them to electrical potential. Just a review, these building blocks are fatty acids DHA, proteins with ECF phospholipid bilayers composed primarily of DHA and minerals bound to carbon molecules. So, what does that mean again: cold water “sea food”, oysters, crustaceans, fish.

Photobiomodulation [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/1900-links-for-pbm-studies.26673](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/1900-links-for-pbm-studies.26673) is huge.

As you know, your sunrise therapy is most critical for programming all your hormones. Make sure you do not expose your skin nor eyes to another light before the sun rises -> this includes your devices. This first light exposure sets your biological clock, light is at the top of Dr. Jack Kruse's protocols.

Bottomline: Raise your voltage, improve your redox

Sharing ideas: [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?posts/297273](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?posts/297273)​

Headaches come and go - seem to be related to sinus issues

Typically this has to do with mycotoxins that have made their way into your sinus

[https://daveasprey.com/why-mycotoxins-are-kryptonite/](https://daveasprey.com/why-mycotoxins-are-kryptonite/)
​

Hair loss has slowed down but still thin

Hair loss or graying is typically a nutritional absorption issue

[https://daveasprey.com/blog-bulletproof-com-hair-loss-regrow-hair-drugs/](https://daveasprey.com/blog-bulletproof-com-hair-loss-regrow-hair-drugs/)
​

Very low leptin (.8 non-fasted)

Understanding your hormone levels is important; however the "science" for handling the results is still experimental by practitioners.​

>
> It is more an indicator of your hormonal metabolism not just leptin, insulin, but the hormones of your adrenals, thyroid, and **pituitary.**
> How do you fix that you may ask?
>
> When our [mitochondria](https://www.nature.com/articles/srep12029) misfire and the voltage potential moves from -200 mV to zero, the cell's voltage flips -> this is the beginning of disease. [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3634644/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3634644/)
>
> In order to heal and repair, our cells’ voltage needs to be greater (negatively speaking) than -50mV. Some doctors say, -200mV. So what's this negative all about, I thought positive is good and negative is bad. <- Nope
>
> When a cell begins its downward spiral, the voltage will move from negative to positive. Once this value passes zero, _[the polarity flips](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3713347/)_. You move from health to disease. So, when your cell's clocking in at or above _[+50mV, it's in cancer](https://cancerci.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s12935-016-0348-8)_ territory.
>
> There three elements for increasing our cellular "Energy" quotient
> · Photoelectric force
> · Biochemical-electrical force / signaling
> · Piezoelectric force
>
> You know by now our photoelectric force comes from <- our sun. Our cells when they are built on the “correct” ingredients are able absorb photons from the sun and convert them to electrical potential. Just a review, these building blocks are fatty acids DHA, proteins with ECF phospholipid bilayers composed primarily of DHA and minerals bound to carbon molecules. So, what does that mean again: cold water “sea food”, oysters, crustaceans, fish.
>
> Photobiomodulation [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/1900-links-for-pbm-studies.26673](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/1900-links-for-pbm-studies.26673) is huge.
>
> As you know, your sunrise therapy is most critical for programming all your hormones. Make sure you do not expose your skin nor eyes to another light before the sun rises -> this includes your devices. This first light exposure sets your biological clock, light is at the top of Dr. Jack Kruse's protocols.
>
>
>
> Bottomline: Raise your voltage, improve your redox
>
> Sharing ideas: [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?posts/297273](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?posts/297273)​

> This is deep but after reading Jack's post on redox, I clearly need to improve my sunlight and my water. I thought fasting blood sugar in the 90's was mostly caused by a high sugar diet the last 35 years of my life. Will this get my fasting blood sugar below 88? How long does it usually take?

Fasting blood sugar is an easy measure, but as you can tell, that doesn't make it easy to fix.

It is more an indicator of your hormonal metabolism not just leptin, insulin, but the hormones of your adrenals, thyroid, and pituitary.
​

How's you "vitamin" D3 & melatonin levels? If they are low even after changing up you sunlight protocols, your liver & kidneys may also not be optimal. If that's you, you may want to consider -

_Photobiomodulation_ [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/1900-links-for-pbm-studies.26673](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/1900-links-for-pbm-studies.26673)

[EMR-Tek](https://emr-tek.com/collections/red-light-therapy/products/firestorm) FireStorm has come to the top of Jack's list -

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/cold-laser-therapy-devices.26518/#post-307386](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/cold-laser-therapy-devices.26518/#post-307386)​

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...y-boost-glutathione-levels.26713/#post-307477](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/how-does-one-naturally-boost-glutathione-levels.26713/#post-307477)
​

_Improving Liver & Kidney function_ falls in the _"Biochemical-electrical force / signaling"_ catagory

One of the things you can try is to improve your COX pathway.
​

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3081099/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3081099/)

Prostaglandin-endoperoxide synthase (PTGS) is an enzyme that is responsible for formation of prostandoids,such as prostacyclin from arachidonic acid.

Prostacyclin: A Vascular Mediator is stimulated by myricetin.

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC1566141/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC1566141/)​

Cyclooxygenase (COX) enzymes are how PTGS make their ATP.

Myricetin stimulates prostacyclin which is a prostandoid.

It has been studied as “A Dietary Molecule with Diverse Biological Activities” -
[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4772053/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4772053/)​

Dihydromyricetin is a form of myricetin.

Dihydromyricetin improves glucose and lipid metabolism and exerts anti-inflammatory effects in nonalcoholic fatty liver disease: A randomized controlled trial -

[https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1043661815000936](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1043661815000936)
​

Recent Update on the Pharmacological Effects and Mechanisms of Dihydromyricetin - [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6209623/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6209623/) The detailed mechanisms may be associated with several different molecules involved in cellular apoptosis, oxidative stress, and inflammation, such as AMP-activated protein kinase (AMPK), mitogen-activated protein kinase (MAPK), protein kinase B (Akt), nuclear factor-κB (NF-κB), nuclear factor E2-related factor 2 (Nrf2), ATP-binding cassette transporter A1 (ABCA1), peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor-γ (PPARγ).

So how do you get your hands on this stuff? ->

[https://herbnutritionals.com/herbal-extracts/dihydromyricetin](https://herbnutritionals.com/herbal-extracts/dihydromyricetin) - consider minimum of 600mg
[https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07RC9G1G9](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07RC9G1G9)​

Back to your question: _How long does it usually take?_

Unknown​

Dr. Hunt, MD, FACOG, practiced obstetrics and gynecology for 15 years before stopping obstetrics to pursue her interest in functional medicine and nutritional genetics. She now focuses on a healing, proactive approach that provides long-term solutions and gets to the root cause to heal chronic illness, with integrative protocols to address a variety of illnesses. She works with both men and women to improve energy, cognition, and hormonal balance.

To work with Dr. Hunt, visit: [https://www.courtneyhuntmd.com...](https://www.courtneyhuntmd.com/)
Instagram: [@courtneyhuntmd](https://www.instagram.com/courtneyhuntmd/)

Dr. Leland Stillman, Integrative Medicine

Dr. Leland Stillman, MD, practices natural and functional medicine with a systematic approach that addresses all aspects of diet, lifestyle, mindset, and environment, to help people achieve optimal health and their highest potential. He takes the best of many disciplines and therapeutic modalities and works with you to develop a comprehensive plan to achieve your health and performance goals. He works with patients all over the world.

> [John Schumacher said:](/goto/post?id=307475)
>
> Fasting blood sugar is an easy measure, but as you can tell, that doesn't make it easy to fix.
>
> It is more an indicator of your hormonal metabolism not just leptin, insulin, but the hormones of your adrenals, thyroid, and pituitary.
> ​
>
> How's you "vitamin" D3 & melatonin levels? If they are low even after changing up you sunlight protocols, your liver & kidneys may also not be optimal. If that's you, you may want to consider -
>
>
> _Photobiomodulation_ [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/1900-links-for-pbm-studies.26673](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/1900-links-for-pbm-studies.26673)
>
> [EMR-Tek](https://emr-tek.com/collections/red-light-therapy/products/firestorm) FireStorm has come to the top of Jack's list -
>
> [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/cold-laser-therapy-devices.26518/#post-307386](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/cold-laser-therapy-devices.26518/#post-307386)​
>
> [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...y-boost-glutathione-levels.26713/#post-307477](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/how-does-one-naturally-boost-glutathione-levels.26713/#post-307477)​
>
> _Improving Liver & Kidney function_ falls in the _"Biochemical-electrical force / signaling"_ catagory
>
>
> One of the things you can try is to improve your COX pathway.
> ​
>
> [View attachment 20046](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/20046/)
> [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3081099/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3081099/)
>
> Prostaglandin-endoperoxide synthase (PTGS) is an enzyme that is responsible for formation of prostandoids,such as prostacyclin from arachidonic acid.
>
> Prostacyclin: A Vascular Mediator is stimulated by myricetin.
>
> [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC1566141/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC1566141/)​
>
> Cyclooxygenase (COX) enzymes are how PTGS make their ATP.
>
> Myricetin stimulates prostacyclin which is a prostandoid.
>
> It has been studied as “A Dietary Molecule with Diverse Biological Activities” -
> [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4772053/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4772053/)​
>
> Dihydromyricetin is a form of myricetin.
>
> Dihydromyricetin improves glucose and lipid metabolism and exerts anti-inflammatory effects in nonalcoholic fatty liver disease: A randomized controlled trial -
>
> [https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1043661815000936](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1043661815000936)
> ​
>
> Recent Update on the Pharmacological Effects and Mechanisms of Dihydromyricetin - [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6209623/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6209623/) The detailed mechanisms may be associated with several different molecules involved in cellular apoptosis, oxidative stress, and inflammation, such as AMP-activated protein kinase (AMPK), mitogen-activated protein kinase (MAPK), protein kinase B (Akt), nuclear factor-κB (NF-κB), nuclear factor E2-related factor 2 (Nrf2), ATP-binding cassette transporter A1 (ABCA1), peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor-γ (PPARγ).
>
> [View attachment 20047](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/20047/)
>
> So how do you get your hands on this stuff? ->
>
> [https://herbnutritionals.com/herbal-extracts/dihydromyricetin](https://herbnutritionals.com/herbal-extracts/dihydromyricetin) - consider minimum of 600mg
> [https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07RC9G1G9](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07RC9G1G9)​
>
>
> Back to your question: _How long does it usually take?_
>
> Unknown​

Wow, lots of information here, thank you. I will study it.

### Penny — 2021-12-16T00:24:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-for-anna-vargo-sleep-irritable-bladder-sinus-headaches.26689/post-307559>

> [Anna Vargo said:](/goto/post?id=307557)
>
> Wow, lots of information here, thank you. I will study it.

Where do you live? Like, northern latitude or southern? Like do you get heavy sunshine or not so much? Stress/blue light/wifi will elevate cortisol which will elevate your blood sugar... so, your environment alone could be doing you in - you need 4 hours of complete darkness to make melatonin and to turn off the insulin pump... how is your sense of smell/taste? I.e. how is your zinc level? If you are in a big city, you are just spinning your wheels - Are you good about no blue light at night and seeing the sunrise/sunset? Tighten up your light - you might consider taking some l-serine and magnesium before bed and maybe a P5P if your zinc is low - I have had amazing luck with oysters as my last meal of the day - consider all the signalling that goes on starting at the brain - you won't even make it to the pituitary to make melatonin if your cell membranes don't have enough DHA - oysters have the perfect ratio of fat/protein plus 200% of the DV of zinc to make sleep wonderful - also, if you are doing caffeine - you are screwing up sleep... how do I know that?  Too much protein at the end of the day is also a fail - always eat protein outside for the UV light - breakfast like a king, lunch like a prince, dinner like a popper I find bone broth is pretty good at taming inflammation at the end of the day also Cheers!
